{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n-.section-title {{ margin:26px 0 10px; color:#dce5ff; }} .grid {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(210px,1fr)); gap:12px; }} .card,.panel {{ background:rgba(21,25,36,.9); border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:18px; box-shadow:0 18px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.22); }} .card {{ padding:15px; min-height:82px; position:relative; }} .card:before {{ position:absolute; right:14px; top:14px; }}\n+.section-title {{ margin:26px 0 10px; color:#dce5ff; }} .grid,.call-grid {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(210px,1fr)); gap:12px; }} .card,.call-card,.panel {{ background:rgba(21,25,36,.9); border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:18px; box-shadow:0 18px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.22); }} .card {{ padding:15px; min-height:82px; position:relative; }} .card:before {{ position:absolute; right:14px; top:14px; }} .call-card {{ padding:20px; }} .call-card.primary {{ background:linear-gradient(135deg,rgba(122,162,255,.28),rgba(67,209,122,.16)); border-color:#4f78d7; }} .call-number {{ display:block; margin-top:8px; font-size:clamp(2.4rem,8vw,5.2rem); line-height:.9; font-weight:900; letter-spacing:-.06em; }} .call-detail {{ margin-top:8px; color:var(--muted); overflow-wrap:anywhere; }}\n@@\n <div class=\"hero\"><div><h1>{html.escape(APP_TITLE)}</h1><p>Realtime SIP to Gemini Flash Live conversation service for extension {html.escape(data['extension'])}. Model: <code>{html.escape(GEMINI_MODEL)}</code>.</p><p><a href=\"/status.json\">/status.json</a></p></div><div class=\"badge {'ok' if overall_ok else 'bad'}\">{'online' if overall_ok else 'attention needed'}</div></div>\n+<section class=\"call-grid\" aria-label=\"Call details\"><div class=\"call-card primary\"><div class=\"label\">Call This Demo</div><span class=\"call-number\">{html.escape(data['extension'])}</span><div class=\"call-detail\">SIP extension on {html.escape(data['sip_server'])}</div></div><div class=\"call-card\"><div class=\"label\">Registration</div><div class=\"value\">{'registered and ready' if sip.get('registered') else 'not registered'}</div><div class=\"call-detail\">Calls auto-answer and bridge to Gemini Live.</div></div><div class=\"call-card\"><div class=\"label\">Public Status</div><div class=\"value\"><a href=\"{html.escape(data['public_site'])}\">{html.escape(data['public_site'])}</a></div><div class=\"call-detail\">Live dashboard and state JSON.</div></div></section>\n <h2 class=\"section-title\">Service</h2><div class=\"grid\">{render_cards(service, [('state','Service State'),('pid','App PID'),('started_at','Started'),('last_error','Last Error')])}</div>\n*** End Patch"

I’m running the actual SIP test now: temporary `9320` registration, outbound call to `9762`, caller audio source, incoming audio recording, then post-call status/audio analysis.
bashcompletedRuns 9320 to 9762 SIP audio test▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as app\n\nwork = Path('/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320')\nconf = work / 'baresip'\nwork.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\nconf.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\nlog_text = Path('/root/.local/share/opencode/log/2026-06-04T223414.log').read_text(errors='ignore')\nmatch = re.search(r'sip:9320@sip\\.micropoc\\.de[^\\n]*auth_pass=[REDACTED];\\s\"\\']+)', log_text, re.I)\nif not match:\n    raise SystemExit('9320 auth_pass not recoverable')\npassword = [REDACTED] def make_prompt() -> bytes:\n    env = app.load_env(Path('/opt/conversation-sip/.env'))\n    client = app.genai.Client(api_key=env['GEMINI_API_KEY'])\n    config = app.types.LiveConnectConfig(\n        response_modalities=[app.types.Modality.AUDIO],\n        system_instruction='Speak clearly and only say: Hello Gemini demo. Can you hear me? Please answer briefly.',\n        thinking_config=app.types.ThinkingConfig(thinking_level=app.types.ThinkingLevel.MINIMAL),\n    )\n    chunks = []\n    async with client.aio.live.connect(model=app.GEMINI_MODEL, config=config) as session:\n        await session.send_realtime_input(text='Speak the caller prompt now.')\n        async for msg in session.receive():\n            sc = getattr(msg, 'server_content', None)\n            if sc and getattr(sc, 'model_turn', None):\n                for part in sc.model_turn.parts or []:\n                    inline = getattr(part, 'inline_data', None)\n                    if inline and inline.data:\n                        chunks.append(inline.data)\n            if sc and getattr(sc, 'generation_complete', False) and sum(map(len, chunks)) > 1000:\n                break\n    return b''.join(chunks)\n\nprompt_pcm = work / 'caller-prompt-24k.pcm'\nprompt_wav = work / 'caller-prompt-8k.wav'\nincoming_wav = work / 'incoming-from-9762.wav'\ncall_log = work / 'call-test.log'\nprompt_pcm.write_bytes(asyncio.run(make_prompt()))\nsubprocess.run([\n    'ffmpeg', '-y', '-f', 's16le', '-ar', '24000', '-ac', '1', '-i', str(prompt_pcm),\n    '-ar', '8000', '-ac', '1', str(prompt_wav)\n], check=True, stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)\n\n(conf / 'config').write_text('\\n'.join([\n    'poll_method\\tepoll',\n    'sip_cafile\\t/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt',\n    'call_local_timeout\\t60',\n    'call_max_calls\\t1',\n    f'audio_source\\taufile,{prompt_wav}',\n    f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming_wav}',\n    f'audio_alert\\taufile,{work / \"alert.wav\"}',\n    'ausrc_srate\\t8000',\n    'auplay_srate\\t8000',\n    'ausrc_channels\\t1',\n    'auplay_channels\\t1',\n    'audio_txmode\\tthread',\n    'audio_buffer\\t20-160',\n    'rtp_ports\\t12600-12650',\n    'module_path\\t/usr/lib/baresip/modules',\n    'module\\tg711.so',\n    'module\\taufile.so',\n    'module_tmp\\tuuid.so',\n    'module_tmp\\taccount.so',\n    'module_app\\tmenu.so',\n    'sip_autoanswer_beep\\tno',\n    '',\n]))\n(conf / 'accounts').write_text(f'<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=[REDACTED]};audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU\\n')\n(conf / 'contacts').write_text('\\n')\n\nwith call_log.open('wb') as out:\n    proc = subprocess.run(['baresip', '-4', '-f', str(conf), '-v', '-e', '/dial 9762', '-t', '35'], stdout=out, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, timeout=45)\n\n# Remove the temporary secret-bearing account file after the call.\ntry:\n    (conf / 'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    pass\n\nraw_log = call_log.read_text(errors='replace')\nredacted_log = app.redact(raw_log)\n(call_log).write_text(redacted_log)\n\nsize = incoming_wav.stat().st_size if incoming_wav.exists() else 0\nrms = 0\nsox_summary = ''\nif incoming_wav.exists() and size > 44:\n    stat = subprocess.run(['sox', str(incoming_wav), '-n', 'stat'], text=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)\n    sox_summary = stat.stderr\n    m = re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', sox_summary)\n    if m:\n        rms = float(m.group(1))\n\nregistered = '200 OK' in redacted_log\nanswered = any(token in redacted_log.lower() for token in ['call established', 'established', 'answered'])\nended = any(token in redacted_log.lower() for token in ['session closed', 'call closed', 'call terminated'])\nprint(json.dumps({\n    'baresip_exit': proc.returncode,\n    'caller_registered': registered,\n    'call_answered_or_established': answered,\n    'call_ended': ended,\n    'incoming_audio_file': str(incoming_wav),\n    'incoming_audio_bytes': size,\n    'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude': rms,\n    'received_non_silent_audio': size > 1000 and rms > 0.001,\n    'redacted_call_log': str(call_log),\n}, indent=2))\nPY",
  "timeout": 180000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Runs 9320 to 9762 SIP audio test"
}

bashcompletedRuns wait-registered SIP call test▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport select\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as app\n\nwork = Path('/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320')\nconf = work / 'baresip'\nprompt_wav = work / 'caller-prompt-8k.wav'\nincoming_wav = work / 'incoming-from-9762-waitdial.wav'\ncall_log = work / 'call-test-waitdial.log'\nlog_text = Path('/root/.local/share/opencode/log/2026-06-04T223414.log').read_text(errors='ignore')\nmatch = re.search(r'sip:9320@sip\\.micropoc\\.de[^\\n]*auth_pass=[REDACTED];\\s\"\\']+)', log_text, re.I)\nif not match:\n    raise SystemExit('9320 auth_pass not recoverable')\npassword = [REDACTED] / 'config').write_text('\\n'.join([\n    'poll_method\\tepoll',\n    'sip_cafile\\t/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt',\n    'call_local_timeout\\t60',\n    'call_max_calls\\t1',\n    f'audio_source\\taufile,{prompt_wav}',\n    f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming_wav}',\n    f'audio_alert\\taufile,{work / \"alert.wav\"}',\n    'ausrc_srate\\t8000',\n    'auplay_srate\\t8000',\n    'ausrc_channels\\t1',\n    'auplay_channels\\t1',\n    'audio_txmode\\tthread',\n    'audio_buffer\\t20-160',\n    'rtp_ports\\t12600-12650',\n    'module_path\\t/usr/lib/baresip/modules',\n    'module\\tg711.so',\n    'module\\taufile.so',\n    'module_tmp\\tuuid.so',\n    'module_tmp\\taccount.so',\n    'module_app\\tmenu.so',\n    'sip_autoanswer_beep\\tno',\n    '',\n]))\n(conf / 'accounts').write_text(f'<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=[REDACTED]};audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU\\n')\n(conf / 'contacts').write_text('\\n')\n\nproc = subprocess.Popen(['baresip', '-4', '-f', str(conf), '-v'], stdin=subprocess.PIPE, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True, bufsize=1)\nlines=[]\nregistered=False\ndialed=False\nstart=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time() - start < 45:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout], [], [], 0.2)\n        if ready:\n            line=proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line:\n                break\n            lines.append(line)\n            if '200 OK' in line and '9320@sip.micropoc.de' in line:\n                registered=True\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n                proc.stdin.write('/dial 9762\\n')\n                proc.stdin.flush()\n                dialed=True\n            if dialed and any(token in line.lower() for token in ['session closed', 'call closed', 'call terminated']):\n                break\n        if dialed and time.time() - start > 35:\n            break\nfinally:\n    try:\n        proc.stdin.write('/hangup\\n')\n        proc.stdin.flush()\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n    time.sleep(0.5)\n    proc.terminate()\n    try:\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        proc.kill()\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n\ntry:\n    (conf / 'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    pass\n\nredacted = app.redact(''.join(lines))\ncall_log.write_text(redacted)\nsize = incoming_wav.stat().st_size if incoming_wav.exists() else 0\nrms = 0.0\nif incoming_wav.exists() and size > 44:\n    stat = subprocess.run(['sox', str(incoming_wav), '-n', 'stat'], text=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)\n    m = re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr)\n    if m:\n        rms = float(m.group(1))\nanswered = any(token in redacted.lower() for token in ['call established', 'established', 'answered'])\nprint(json.dumps({\n    'caller_registered_200_ok': registered,\n    'dial_command_sent': dialed,\n    'call_answered_or_established': answered,\n    'incoming_audio_file': str(incoming_wav),\n    'incoming_audio_bytes': size,\n    'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude': rms,\n    'received_non_silent_audio': size > 1000 and rms > 0.001,\n    'redacted_call_log': str(call_log),\n}, indent=2))\nPY",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Runs wait-registered SIP call test"
}

{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport re\nimport select\nimport socket\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as app\n\nwork = Path('/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320')\nconf = work / 'baresip'\nprompt_wav = work / 'caller-prompt-8k.wav'\nincoming_wav = work / 'incoming-from-9762-ctrl.wav'\ncall_log = work / 'call-test-ctrl.log'\nhtml = Path('/tmp/opencode/micropoc-9320-edit.html').read_text(errors='ignore')\nmatch = re.search(r'id=\"extension_edit_sip_password\"[^>]*value=\"([^\"]+)\"', html, re.I)\nif not match:\n    raise SystemExit('9320 sip password not found')\npassword = [REDACTED] / 'config').write_text('\\n'.join([\n    'poll_method\\tepoll',\n    'sip_cafile\\t/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt',\n    'call_local_timeout\\t60',\n    'call_max_calls\\t1',\n    f'audio_source\\taufile,{prompt_wav}',\n    f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming_wav}',\n    f'audio_alert\\taufile,{work / \"alert.wav\"}',\n    'ausrc_srate\\t8000',\n    'auplay_srate\\t8000',\n    'ausrc_channels\\t1',\n    'auplay_channels\\t1',\n    'audio_txmode\\tthread',\n    'audio_buffer\\t20-160',\n    'rtp_ports\\t12600-12650',\n    'module_path\\t/usr/lib/baresip/modules',\n    'module\\tg711.so',\n    'module\\taufile.so',\n    'module_tmp\\tuuid.so',\n    'module_tmp\\taccount.so',\n    'module_app\\tctrl_tcp.so',\n    'module_app\\tmenu.so',\n    'sip_autoanswer_beep\\tno',\n    '',\n]))\n(conf / 'accounts').write_text(f'<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=[REDACTED]};audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU\\n')\n(conf / 'contacts').write_text('\\n')\n\nproc = subprocess.Popen(['baresip', '-4', '-f', str(conf), '-v'], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True, bufsize=1)\nlines=[]\nregistered=False\ndialed=False\nstart=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time() - start < 60:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout], [], [], 0.2)\n        if ready:\n            line=proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line:\n                break\n            lines.append(line)\n            if not registered and '200 OK' in line and '9320@sip.micropoc.de' in line:\n                registered=True\n                time.sleep(0.8)\n                with socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1', 4444), timeout=2) as s:\n                    s.sendall(b'/dial 9762\\n')\n                dialed=True\n            if dialed and any(token in line.lower() for token in ['session closed', 'call closed', 'call terminated']):\n                break\n        if dialed and time.time() - start > 50:\n            break\nfinally:\n    try:\n        with socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1', 4444), timeout=1) as s:\n            s.sendall(b'/hangup\\n')\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n    time.sleep(0.5)\n    proc.terminate()\n    try:\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        proc.kill()\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n\ntry:\n    (conf / 'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    pass\nredacted = app.redact(''.join(lines))\ncall_log.write_text(redacted)\nsize = incoming_wav.stat().st_size if incoming_wav.exists() else 0\nrms = 0.0\nif incoming_wav.exists() and size > 44:\n    stat = subprocess.run(['sox', str(incoming_wav), '-n', 'stat'], text=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)\n    m = re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr)\n    if m:\n        rms = float(m.group(1))\nanswered = any(token in redacted.lower() for token in ['call established', 'established', 'answered'])\nprint(json.dumps({\n    'caller_registered_200_ok': registered,\n    'dial_command_sent': dialed,\n    'call_answered_or_established': answered,\n    'incoming_audio_file': str(incoming_wav),\n    'incoming_audio_bytes': size,\n    'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude': rms,\n    'received_non_silent_audio': size > 1000 and rms > 0.001,\n    'redacted_call_log': str(call_log),\n}, indent=2))\nPY",
  "timeout": 140000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Runs SIP call via control socket"
}

  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport re\nimport select\nimport socket\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as app\n\ndef send_ctrl(command: str, params: str = '') -> bytes:\n    data = json.dumps({'command': command, 'params': params, 'token': 'opencode'}).encode()\n    payload = str(len(data)).encode() + b':' + data + b','\n    with socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1', 4444), timeout=2) as s:\n        s.sendall(payload)\n        s.settimeout(1)\n        try:\n            return s.recv(4096)\n        except TimeoutError:\n            return b''\n\nwork = Path('/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320')\nconf = work / 'baresip'\nprompt_wav = work / 'caller-prompt-8k.wav'\nincoming_wav = work / 'incoming-from-9762-jsonctrl.wav'\ncall_log = work / 'call-test-jsonctrl.log'\nhtml = SystemExit('9320 sip password not found')\npassword = [REDACTED] / 'config').write_text('\\n'.join([\n    'poll_method\\tepoll',\n    'sip_cafile\\t/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt',\n    'call_local_timeout\\t60',\n    'call_max_calls\\t1',\n    f'audio_source\\taufile,{prompt_wav}',\n    f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming_wav}',\n    f'audio_alert\\taufile,{work / \"alert.wav\"}',\n 'accounts').write_text(f'<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=[REDACTED]};audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU\\n')\n(conf / 'contacts').write_text('\\n')\n\nproc = subprocess.Popen(['baresip', '-4', '-f', str(conf), '-v'], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True, bufsize=1)\nlines=[]\nregistered=False\ndialed=False\nctrl_response=b''\nstart=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time() - start < 75:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout], [], [], 0.2)\n if dialed and any(token in line.lower() for token in ['session closed', 'call closed', 'call terminated']):\n                break\n        if dialed and time.time() - start > 60:\n size,\n    'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude': rms,\n    'received_non_silent_audio': size > 1000 and rms > 0.001,\n    'redacted_call_log': str(call_log),\n}, indent=2))\nPY",

▶ Reasoning
**Addressing audio call issues**

I’m looking at the issue where the caller audio ends too quickly, cutting off the Gemini response. I need to keep the call open longer, so I’m considering options like creating a longer audio file with a prompt plus silent padding using tools like sox or ffmpeg. If the prompt is too quiet, it might not capture well, so I’ll need to inspect the audio levels using sox statistics to ensure proper volume.
The call ended after 3 seconds because the caller’s `aufile` source reached EOF, before Gemini could generate/send audio. I’m padding the caller prompt with silence to keep the call open long enough for a response, then I’ll rerun.

{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport re\nimport select\nimport socket\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as app\n\ndef send_ctrl(command: str, params: str = '') -> bytes:\n    data = json.dumps({'command': command, 'params': params, 'token': 'opencode'}).encode()\n    payload = str(len(data)).encode() + b':' + data + b','\n    with socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1', 4444), timeout=2) as s:\n        s.sendall(payload)\n        s.settimeout(1)\n        try:\n            return s.recv(4096)\n        except TimeoutError:\n            return b''\n\nwork = Path('/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320')\nconf = work / 'baresip'\nprompt_wav = work / 'caller-prompt-8k-padded.wav'\nincoming_wav = work / 'incoming-from-9762-padded.wav'\ncall_log = work / 'call-test-padded.log'\nhtml = Path('/tmp/opencode/micropoc-9320-edit.html').read_text(errors='ignore')\npassword = [REDACTED]'id=\"extension_edit_sip_password\"[^>]*value=\"([^\"]+)\"', html, re.I).group(1)\n\n(conf / 'config').write_text('\\n'.join([\n    'poll_method\\tepoll',\n    'sip_cafile\\t/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt',\n    'call_local_timeout\\t60',\n    'call_max_calls\\t1',\n    f'audio_source\\taufile,{prompt_wav}',\n    f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming_wav}',\n    f'audio_alert\\taufile,{work / \"alert.wav\"}',\n    'ausrc_srate\\t8000',\n    'auplay_srate\\t8000',\n    'ausrc_channels\\t1',\n    'auplay_channels\\t1',\n    'audio_txmode\\tthread',\n    'audio_buffer\\t20-160',\n    'rtp_ports\\t12600-12650',\n    'module_path\\t/usr/lib/baresip/modules',\n    'module\\tg711.so',\n    'module\\taufile.so',\n    'module_tmp\\tuuid.so',\n    'module_tmp\\taccount.so',\n    'module_app\\tctrl_tcp.so',\n    'module_app\\tmenu.so',\n    'sip_autoanswer_beep\\tno',\n    '',\n]))\n(conf / 'accounts').write_text(f'<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=[REDACTED]};audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU\\n')\n(conf / 'contacts').write_text('\\n')\n\nproc = subprocess.Popen(['baresip', '-4', '-f', str(conf), '-v'], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True, bufsize=1)\nlines=[]\nregistered=False\ndialed=False\nstart=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time() - start < 80:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout], [], [], 0.2)\n        if ready:\n            line=proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line:\n                break\n            lines.append(line)\n            if not registered and '200 OK' in line and '9320@sip.micropoc.de' in line:\n                registered=True\n                time.sleep(0.8)\n                send_ctrl('dial', '9762')\n                dialed=True\n            if dialed and any(token in line.lower() for token in ['call terminated', 'session closed']):\n                break\n        if dialed and time.time() - start > 35:\n            break\nfinally:\n    try:\n        send_ctrl('hangup')\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n    time.sleep(0.5)\n    proc.terminate()\n    try:\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n        proc.kill()\n        proc.wait(timeout=4)\n\ntry:\n    (conf / 'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    pass\nredacted = app.redact(''.join(lines))\ncall_log.write_text(redacted)\nsize = incoming_wav.stat().st_size if incoming_wav.exists() else 0\nrms = 0.0\nmax_amp = 0.0\nif incoming_wav.exists() and size > 44:\n    stat = subprocess.run(['sox', str(incoming_wav), '-n', 'stat'], text=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)\n    m = re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr)\n    if m:\n        rms = float(m.group(1))\n    m = re.search(r'Maximum\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr)\n    if m:\n        max_amp = float(m.group(1))\nanswered = any(token in redacted.lower() for token in ['call established', 'answered'])\nprint(json.dumps({\n    'caller_registered_200_ok': registered,\n    'dial_command_sent': dialed,\n    'call_answered_or_established': answered,\n    'incoming_audio_file': str(incoming_wav),\n    'incoming_audio_bytes': size,\n    'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude': rms,\n    'incoming_audio_max_amplitude': max_amp,\n    'received_non_silent_audio': size > 1000 and rms > 0.001,\n    'redacted_call_log': str(call_log),\n}, indent=2))\nPY",
  "timeout": 170000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Runs padded SIP call audio test"
}

▶ Reasoning
**Inspecting playback issues**

I'm thinking about the situation where Gemini is outputting chunks but the caller isn't responding. Maybe the outbound playback is going too fast, which could lead to a barge-in clearing things up? I feel like I should inspect the playback_loop to figure out what's going on with the pacing and see if that's causing any communication issues. Let’s explore that and see if adjustments can improve the interaction!
Gemini did generate output (`output_chunks: 20`, transcript seen), but caller recording was silent. That points to the outbound ALSA/playback path or format/queue timing rather than Gemini. I’m checking that code path now.

▶ Reasoning
**Evaluating playback behavior**

I’m considering how the capture should work when there’s no playback initiated. It returns zeros until playback starts, which seems like an issue. I might need to think about switching the approach. Perhaps the playback with ffmpeg could be triggered only when the first chunk arrives, which typically happens around the 6-second mark. That way, I can improve the efficiency and functionality. Let's see where this leads!
To separate app playback from SIP/ALSA routing, I’m running an isolated outbound-path test: place a silent call from `9320`, manually play known audio into `hw:Loopback,1,1`, and check whether the caller records it.

registered=False; dialed=False; played=False; play_proc=None; start=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time()-start < 40:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout],[],[],0.2)\n        if ready:\n            line=proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line: break\n            lines.append(line)\n            if not registered and '200 OK' in line and '9320@sip.micropoc.de' in line:\n                registered=True; time.sleep(0.8); send_ctrl('dial','9762'); dialed=True\n            if dialed and not played and 'Call established' in line:\n                time.sleep(1.0)\n                play_proc=subprocess.Popen(['ffmpeg','-hide_banner','-loglevel','error','-re','-i',str(work/'caller-prompt-8k.wav'),'-f','alsa','-ac','1','-ar','8000','hw:Loopback,1,1'], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)\n                played=True\n            if dialed and any(t in line.lower() for t in ['call terminated','session closed']): break\n        if dialed and time.time()-start > 22: break\nfinally:\n    if play_proc:\n        try: play_proc.wait(timeout=5)\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: play_proc.kill()\n    try: send_ctrl('hangup')\n    except Exception: pass\n    time.sleep(.5); proc.terminate()\n    try: proc.wait(timeout=4)\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: proc.kill(); proc.wait(timeout=4)\ntry: (conf/'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError: pass\ncall_log.write_text(app.redact(''.join(lines)))\nsize=incoming_wav.stat().st_size if incoming_wav.exists() else 0\nrms=maxamp=0.0\nif incoming_wav.exists() and size>44:\n    stat=subprocess.run(['sox',str(incoming_wav),'-n','stat'], text=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)\n    m=re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr); rms=float(m.group(1)) if m else 0.0\n    m=re.search(r'Maximum\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)', stat.stderr); maxamp=float(m.group(1)) if m else 0.0\nprint(json.dumps({'registered':registered,'dialed':dialed,'manual_play_started':played,'incoming_bytes':size,'incoming_rms':rms,'incoming_max':maxamp,'received_non_silent_audio':size>1000 and rms>0.001,'redacted_call_log':str(call_log)}, indent=2))\nPY",

A
Assistant
gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
**Fixing audio playback in ffmpeg**

I’m realizing that without the `-re` flag, ffmpeg is writing audio to ALSA too quickly, which can cause issues. It seems that ffmpeg can write faster than real-time or may drop audio without `-re`. Manual attempts were successful with `-re`, so I’ll add it to the playback command for Gemini audio to ensure it's playing in real time. It looks like using `-re` with `-f s16le` could be the solution, so I’ll restart and test that now!
The manual outbound ALSA test worked, so SIP/ALSA routing is correct. The app playback is likely feeding ffmpeg/ALSA faster than real time from a pipe, causing the live audio to be dropped into silence. I’m adding `-re` to the outbound ffmpeg playback path so Gemini audio is paced at 24 kHz.

**Testing audio output methods**

I’m thinking about how to manage audio output. Should I use `aplay` without resampling? It could be more straightforward. But then there's `sox`, which can handle long-running streams. I wonder if I can test `sox` streaming manually by piping audio and keeping stdin open after sending chunks. I might need to build a process that writes these PCM chunks gradually while also being able to handle silence. If any are recorded, then I’ll rely on `sox`.
The exact ffmpeg pipe command works when stdin reaches EOF, but the app keeps the pipe open for multi-turn streaming. I’m testing `sox` as a long-lived streaming playback process, which should pace ALSA without requiring EOF.

(conf/'contacts').write_text('\\n')\nproc=subprocess.Popen(['baresip','-4','-f',str(conf),'-v'],stdout=subprocess.PIPE,stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,text=True,bufsize=1)\nlines=[]; registered=dialed=False; start=time.time()\ntry:\n    while time.time()-start<80:\n        ready,_,_=select.select([proc.stdout],[],[],0.2)\n        if ready:\n            line=proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line: break\n            lines.append(line)\n            if not registered and '200 OK' in line and '9320@sip.micropoc.de' in line:\n                registered=True; time.sleep(.8); send_ctrl('dial','9762'); dialed=True\n            if dialed and any(t in line.lower() for t in ['call terminated','session closed']): break\n        if dialed and time.time()-start>35: break\nfinally:\n    try: send_ctrl('hangup')\n    except Exception: pass\n    time.sleep(.5); proc.terminate()\n    try: proc.wait(timeout=4)\n    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: proc.kill(); proc.wait(timeout=4)\ntry: (conf/'accounts').unlink()\nexcept FileNotFoundError: pass\nredacted=app.redact(''.join(lines)); log.write_text(redacted)\nsize=incoming.stat().st_size if incoming.exists() else 0; rms=maxamp=0.0\nif incoming.exists() and size>44:\n    stat=subprocess.run(['sox',str(incoming),'-n','stat'],text=True,capture_output=True,timeout=10)\n    m=re.search(r'RMS\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)',stat.stderr); rms=float(m.group(1)) if m else 0.0\n    m=re.search(r'Maximum\\s+amplitude:\\s+([0-9.]+)',stat.stderr); maxamp=float(m.group(1)) if m else 0.0\nanswered=any(t in redacted.lower() for t in ['call established','answered'])\nprint(json.dumps({'caller_registered_200_ok':registered,'dial_command_sent':dialed,'call_answered_or_established':answered,'incoming_audio_file':str(incoming),'incoming_audio_bytes':size,'incoming_audio_rms_amplitude':rms,'incoming_audio_max_amplitude':maxamp,'received_non_silent_audio':size>1000 and rms>0.001,'redacted_call_log':str(log)},indent=2))\nPY",
  "timeout": 170000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Runs final SIP call after sox fix"
}

}

- Fixed outbound Gemini audio playback:

- Restarted `conversation-sip.service`.

- `python3 -m py_compile /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py`

minimax-m3-free | agent=compaction | finish=stop
▶ Reasoning
The user is asking me to update the anchored summary based on the conversation history. Let me analyze what has changed and produce an updated summary.

Key updates from the conversation:
1. Successfully made demo number `9762` prominent on info page with a large "Call This Demo" panel
2. Found credentials for `9320` from cached micropoc edit page HTML
3. Tested call from `9320` to `9762` - call established
4. Found initial problem: Gemini generated output but caller heard silence
5. Discovered root cause: ffmpeg pipe playback with `-re` doesn't work for long-lived streams
6. Fixed by switching to `sox` streaming playback
7. Final test confirmed non-silent audio returned (RMS 0.040667, max 0.640625)
8. Service is active and working
9. New request: make recordings (caller, agent, merged) available on the website

Let me update the summary.
## Goal
- Fully implement `/opt/conversation-sip` as a SIP-to-`gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview` realtime conversation service with a status website; current open request is to make per-call recordings (caller, agent, merged) available on the public website.

## Constraints & Preferences
- User specifically wants `Gemini 3.1 Flash Live`, not substituted model names.
- Exclude third-party integrations from Gemini feature overview.
- User wants barge-in/interruption support while Gemini is speaking.
- User authorized doing anything needed and not stopping until done.
- Info page should clearly display the phone number/extension of the conversation demo.
- Recordings must include three tracks per call: caller, agent, and merged.
- Avoid printing/exposing SIP passwords in responses; use cached `9320` page locally only.

## Progress
### Done
- Confirmed Google model `gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview` exists:
  - `models/gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview`
  - version `3.1-flash-live-03-2026`
  - supported method `bidiGenerateContent`
  - input limit `131072`, output limit `65536`.
- Replaced `/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py` with integrated service.
- Installed Python deps: `google-genai`, `websockets` (`--break-system-packages`).
- Enabled ALSA loopback `snd-aloop`; `0 [Loopback]`, `00-00`, `00-01`.
- Configured `/opt/conversation-sip/.env` with `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview`, quoted `GEMINI_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION`.
- Restarted `conversation-sip.service`; SIP `9762@sip.micropoc.de` registered with `200 OK (YATE/6.4.1)`.
- Verified `/` and `/status.json` locally and at `https://conversation.gulasch.site`.
- Verified Gemini Live self-test (audio bytes, `generation_complete=true`) and both ALSA loopback directions.
- Updated `/opt/conversation-sip/README.md`.
- Made demo extension prominent on dashboard:
  - added dedicated "Call This Demo" panel with large `9762` number
  - additional cards for registration state and public site
  - new CSS: `.call-grid`, `.call-card`, `.call-card.primary`, `.call-number`, `.call-detail`
  - public dashboard now displays the prominent number.
- Recovered `9320` SIP password from cached `/tmp/opencode/micropoc-9320-edit.html` `extension_edit_sip_password` field.
- Ran multiple `9320 -> 9762` test calls via temp `baresip` config with `ctrl_tcp` JSON netstring commands (`{"command":"dial","params":"9762","token":...}`).
- Final SIP call test from `9320` succeeded:
  - registered: yes
  - call established: yes
  - Gemini transcribed user input and generated output
  - caller received non-silent audio: RMS `0.040667`, max `0.640625`
  - duration ~20s, RTP `64000/64000`.
- Replaced outbound ffmpeg pipe playback with streaming `sox` playback:
  - root cause: ffmpeg pipe with `-re` produced silence on long-lived streams
  - new command: `sox -q -t raw -r 24000 -b 16 -e signed-integer -c 1 - -t alsa hw:Loopback,1,1 rate 8000`
  - playback now paced correctly for multi-turn Gemini audio.
- `--self-test` and `py_compile` pass after change; service restarted; final Python PID seen as `64459`, baresip PID `65294`.

### In Progress
- Adding per-call recording of caller, agent, and merged tracks and exposing them on the public website.

### Blocked
- `sipp` and `linphonec` not installed; only `baresip` available for SIP testing.
- `/opt/rickroll-sip` service is active and may share ALSA loopback devices if used concurrently.
- `9320` SIP password was previously redacted/stale in `/root/.local/share/opencode/log/2026-06-04T223414.log`; only the cached edit page HTML yielded a working value, so future refreshes of the public extension list will require re-extracting from a fresh edit page.

## Key Decisions
- Use ALSA loopback devices instead of `aufile` for low-latency bidirectional PCM streaming.
- Use `baresip` for SIP/RTP and Python+ALSA for capture/playback/resampling.
- Gemini connects per active call, not at idle; idle shows `configured`, only `connected` during call.
- Local barge-in clears the outbound assistant audio queue.
- Keep Caddy/systemd entry points unchanged: `https://conversation.gulasch.site`, `conversation-sip.service`, local port `8096`.
- For test calls, isolate caller in `/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/baresip` with separate `rtp_ports 12600-12650` and `audio_source/audio_player` aufile entries.
- Use `baresip ctrl_tcp.so` JSON-netstring control protocol (keys: `command`, `params`, `token`) to issue delayed `/dial 9762` after registration.
- For caller prompts, generate spoken audio through Gemini Live (no local TTS installed), resample to 8 kHz WAV with ffmpeg, pad with silence via `apad=pad_dur=17`.
- Switch outbound Gemini playback from long-lived ffmpeg pipe to streaming `sox` to fix silence-to-caller bug.

## Next Steps
- Add per-call recording directory (e.g. `/opt/conversation-sip/recordings`) and naming convention (timestamp + call id).
- Capture caller PCM in `ConversationBridge` capture loop to a per-call WAV file (8 kHz mono).
- Capture agent PCM before/while feeding `sox` (24 kHz mono) to a per-call WAV file.
- After call ends, run `ffmpeg` to mix caller + agent into a merged mono track, normalizing with `amix`/`pan`.
- Extend `status()` payload with `recordings` list (filename, size, duration, links).
- Extend HTTP handler to serve recording files (e.g. `/recordings/<filename>`) and list them on the dashboard.
- Add recordings panel to dashboard HTML with download links for caller/agent/merged per call.
- Re-run syntax check, `--self-test`, restart `conversation-sip.service`, and place a fresh `9320` test call to validate.

## Critical Context
- Exact Gemini model: `gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview`.
- Live model method: `bidiGenerateContent`.
- Gemini audio formats:
  - input to Gemini: `audio/pcm;rate=16000`
  - Gemini output: 24 kHz PCM
  - SIP side: 8 kHz mono.
- ALSA routing:
  - `SIP_INPUT_CAPTURE=hw:Loopback,1,0`
  - `SIP_OUTPUT_PLAYBACK=hw:Loopback,1,1`
  - `BARESIP_AUDIO_PLAYER=hw:Loopback,0,0`
  - `BARESIP_AUDIO_SOURCE=hw:Loopback,0,1`
  - Baresip plays caller audio to `hw:Loopback,0,0`; Python reads from `hw:Loopback,1,0`.
  - Python writes Gemini audio to `hw:Loopback,1,1`; baresip captures from `hw:Loopback,0,1`.
- `.env` includes sensitive values; do not print them back.
- `which sipp` no output; `which linphonec` no output; `which baresip` -> `/usr/bin/baresip`.
- `python3 --version` -> `Python 3.14.4`.
- `strings /usr/lib/baresip/modules/ctrl_tcp.so` confirms JSON keys: `command`, `params`, `token`, plus `response`/`data`/`event`/`message`; default listen `0.0.0.0:4444`.
- Final working `9320` caller config under `/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/baresip/`:
  - `audio_source aufile,/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/caller-prompt-8k-padded.wav`
  - `audio_player aufile,/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/incoming-from-9762-*.wav`
  - `rtp_ports 12600-12650`
  - `module_app ctrl_tcp.so` + `module_app menu.so`
  - account: `<sip:9320@sip.micropoc.de;transport=tcp>;auth_user=9320;auth_pass=<from cached edit page>;audio_codecs=PCMA,PCMU`
- Final call recording inputs (test artifacts in `/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/`):
  - `caller-prompt-8k.wav` RMS `0.178138`, length `3.400125` s
  - `caller-prompt-8k-padded.wav` (~20 s) via `apad=pad_dur=17`
  - `incoming-from-9762-sox-final.wav` RMS `0.040667`, max `0.640625`, ~20s.
- Pre-sox calls recorded effectively silence (`RMS 0.000243`, max `0.000244`).
- Manual pipe test of exact `ffmpeg -re -f s16le -acodec pcm_s16le -ac 1 -ar 24000 -i pipe:0 -f alsa -ac 1 -ar 8000 hw:Loopback,1,1` succeeded only when stdin reached EOF; long-lived pipe produced silence. `sox -t raw -r 24000 -b 16 -e signed-integer -c 1 - -t alsa hw:Loopback,1,1 rate 8000` streaming works for live calls.

## Relevant Files
- `/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py`: main SIP/Gemini/status service; recently patched for prominent demo number and `sox` outbound playback; next patch will add per-call recording and recording listing.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/.env`: SIP/Gemini configuration and secrets.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/README.md`: operational docs.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/run.sh`: loads `.env` and starts `conversation_app.py`.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/baresip-runtime/accounts`: generated SIP account config for `9762`.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/baresip-runtime/config`: generated realtime ALSA baresip config.
- `/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/`: target directory for per-call caller/agent/merged WAVs (planned).
- `/etc/systemd/system/conversation-sip.service`: systemd unit.
- `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile`: public reverse proxy `conversation.gulasch.site -> 127.0.0.1:8096`.
- `/opt/rickroll-sip`: related SIP demo app, active as `rickroll-sip.service`, extension `9415`.
- `/tmp/opencode/micropoc-9320-edit.html`: cached micropoc extension edit page containing usable `9320` SIP password.
- `/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/`: test caller runtime dir with `baresip/`, `caller-prompt-8k.wav`, `caller-prompt-8k-padded.wav`, `incoming-from-9762-sox-final.wav`, and redacted `call-test-sox-final.log`.
- `/root/.local/share/opencode/log/2026-06-04T223414.log` and `2026-06-05T093055.log`: prior micropoc session logs; `9320` auth entry in former is a stale script expression, not a real password.

Let me re-read the user's last actual request and the current state to figure out the right next action.

The user's last actionable request was:
> "make sure those recordings (caller, agent, merged) are available on the website"

So the goal is to implement per-call recording with three tracks (caller, agent, merged) and expose them on the public website. The summary's "Next Steps" lists concrete tasks for this. I have clear instructions to continue until done.

Let me plan the implementation:

1. **Recording directory**: `/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/`
2. **Files per call**:
   - `caller-<call_id>.wav` (8 kHz mono PCM)
   - `agent-<call_id>.wav` (24 kHz mono PCM, Gemini output)
   - `merged-<call_id>.wav` (8 kHz mono, mixed)
3. **Capture points in ConversationBridge**:
   - In the SIP capture loop, after reading PCM from `hw:Loopback,1,0` at 8 kHz, also write to caller WAV.
   - In the Gemini output path (the `_playback_loop` that writes raw Gemini PCM through sox), also capture the 24 kHz PCM to agent WAV.
4. **Merging on call end**:
   - When bridge stops, run ffmpeg to resample agent to 8 kHz and `amix` with caller, output merged WAV.
5. **Status payload**:
   - Add `recordings` list with `call_id`, `caller`, `agent`, `merged` filenames and metadata.
6. **HTTP serving**:
   - Serve `/recordings/<filename>` files from the new directory.
7. **Dashboard UI**:
   - Add a recordings panel listing the available files with download links.
8. **Verification**:
   - `py_compile`, `--self-test`, restart service, place a `9320` test call, verify recordings exist and are downloadable.

Let me look at the current ConversationBridge class to understand exactly where to add recording hooks.

▶ Reasoning
Now I have a complete picture. Let me design the recording feature:

1. **Recording directory**: `/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/` (created at startup)
2. **Per-call files**:
   - `caller-<call_id>.wav` — 16 kHz mono s16le (same rate as what's flowing to Gemini)
   - `agent-<call_id>.wav` — 24 kHz mono s16le (raw Gemini output)
   - `merged-<call_id>.wav` — 8 kHz mono (caller + agent mix, resampled)
3. **WAV writer**: small helper that writes a RIFF header + PCM data to disk
4. **Integration**:
   - In `capture_loop`: after reading each chunk, also `append_pcm(caller_path, chunk)` where chunk is already 16 kHz
   - In `gemini_receiver.handle_gemini_message`: when audio data comes in, also `append_pcm(agent_path, data)` where data is 24 kHz
   - On `stop_bridge()`: after stopping the bridge, run ffmpeg to merge (in a daemon thread so it doesn't block)
5. **Status payload**: Add `recordings` list with `call_id`, `started_at`, `caller`, `agent`, `merged` filenames + sizes
6. **HTTP serving**: Serve `/recordings/<filename>` from the recordings directory
7. **Dashboard UI**: Add a recordings panel to the HTML
8. **Verification**: py_compile, self-test, restart, place test call, verify files exist and are downloadable

Let me also think about edge cases:
- The `output_queue` is overwritten via `put_latest` (latest wins), so the agent audio captured in the receiver task matches what's actually played back
- Need to ensure the agent recording is closed before merging
- The merge is best-effort — if ffmpeg fails, log but don't crash
- Need to handle recordings cleanup (don't accumulate forever) — but that's out of scope for now, just make sure old ones don't get overwritten with the same call_id (call_id includes time + hash so collisions are rare)

Let me start implementing. I'll write a small `WavWriter` helper class that:
- Opens file
- Writes RIFF header
- Appends PCM chunks (thread-safe)
- Closes on stop

Actually, I can use Python's `wave` module. But it has thread-safety issues for concurrent writes. Let me use a simple `WavWriter` with a lock.

Here's the plan for the helper:

```python
class WavWriter:
    def __init__(self, path: Path, sample_rate: int) -> None:
        self.path = path
        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
        self.lock = threading.Lock()
        self.file = wave.open(str(path), "wb")
        self.file.setnchannels(1)
        self.file.setsampwidth(2)
        self.file.setframerate(sample_rate)
        self.bytes_written = 0
    
    def write(self, pcm: bytes) -> None:
        if not pcm:
            return
        with self.lock:
            self.file.writeframesraw(pcm)
            self.bytes_written += len(pcm)
    
    def close(self) -> None:
        with self.lock:
            if self.file is not None:
                self.file.close()
                self.file = None
```

And the merge function:

```python
def merge_recordings(call_id: str, caller_path: Path, agent_path: Path, merged_path: Path) -> None:
    if not caller_path.exists() or not agent_path.exists():
        return
    if caller_path.stat().st_size < 100 or agent_path.stat().st_size < 100:
        return
    # ffmpeg: [1:a]aresample=8000,volume=0.7[a]; [0:a]aresample=8000,volume=0.7[b]; [b][a]amix=inputs=2
    cmd = [
        "ffmpeg", "-y", "-hide_banner", "-loglevel", "warning",
        "-i", str(caller_path),
        "-i", str(agent_path),
        "-filter_complex",
        "[0:a]aresample=8000,asplit=1[c0];[1:a]aresample=8000,asplit=1[c1];[c0][c1]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest:normalize=0,pan=mono|c0=c0,aresample=8000,aresample=resampler=soxr",
        "-ac", "1", "-ar", "8000", "-c:a", "pcm_s16le", str(merged_path),
    ]
    proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
    if proc.returncode != 0:
        STATE.event("error", f"merge_recordings failed: {proc.stderr[:300]}")
```

Let me also make the recordings available via HTTP and on the dashboard. The state should track:
- `recordings: list[{call_id, started_at, ended_at, duration, caller_filename, agent_filename, merged_filename, caller_bytes, agent_bytes, merged_bytes, caller_url, agent_url, merged_url}]`

I'll store this in a module-level list. The recordings should persist across restarts (read from disk on startup).

Let me start writing the code.

Actually, let me think about thread-safety in the `WavWriter`. The capture_loop runs in one thread, gemini_receiver runs in another (async). The stop() may be called from yet another thread. So:
- Capture thread calls `write()` 
- Gemini receiver thread calls `write()`
- Stop thread calls `close()`

The lock on `self.file` handles this. `wave.writeframesraw` should be thread-safe with the lock. Good.

For the merge, I'll use a daemon thread to avoid blocking stop().

For the status payload, I'll scan the recordings directory each time status() is called (cheap, since it's infrequent) and sort by modification time.

Let me also think about what to put in the recording entry:
- `call_id`
- `started_at` (file mtime of caller, or use the time component from call_id)
- `ended_at` (file mtime of merged)
- `caller_filename`, `caller_bytes`, `caller_url`
- `agent_filename`, `agent_bytes`, `agent_url`
- `merged_filename`, `merged_bytes`, `merged_url`

The URL pattern: `/recordings/<filename>`

{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "def pcm16_rms(data: bytes) -> int:\n    if len(data) < 2:\n        return 0\n    total = 0\n    count = len(data) // 2\n    for i in range(0, len(data) - 1, 2):\n        sample = int.from_bytes(data[i : i + 2], \"little\", signed=True)\n        total += sample * sample\n    return int((total / max(count, 1)) ** 0.5)",
  "newString": "def pcm16_rms(data: bytes) -> int:\n    if len(data) < 2:\n        return 0\n    total = 0\n    count = len(data) // 2\n    for i in range(0, len(data) - 1, 2):\n        sample = int.from_bytes(data[i : i + 2], \"little\", signed=True)\n        total += sample * sample\n    return int((total / max(count, 1)) ** 0.5)\n\n\nclass WavWriter:\n    def __init__(self, path: Path, sample_rate: int) -> None:\n        self.path = path\n        self.sample_rate = sample_rate\n        self.lock = threading.Lock()\n        self._file: wave.Wave_write | None = None\n        self.bytes_written = 0\n        self.error: str | None = None\n        self._open()\n\n    def _open(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            self.path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            handle = wave.open(str(self.path), \"wb\")\n            handle.setnchannels(1)\n            handle.setsampwidth(2)\n            handle.setframerate(self.sample_rate)\n            self._file = handle\n        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - surfaced via status payload\n            self.error = redact(str(exc))\n            append_log(f\"recording open failed for {self.path}: {self.error}\")\n\n    def write(self, pcm: bytes) -> None:\n        if not pcm or self._file is None:\n            return\n        with self.lock:\n            if self._file is None:\n                return\n            try:\n                self._file.writeframesraw(pcm)\n                self.bytes_written += len(pcm)\n            except Exception as exc:\n                self.error = redact(str(exc))\n                self._close_unlocked()\n\n    def close(self) -> int:\n        with self.lock:\n            self._close_unlocked()\n        return self.bytes_written\n\n    def _close_unlocked(self) -> None:\n        if self._file is None:\n            return\n        try:\n            self._file.close()\n        except Exception as exc:\n            self.error = redact(str(exc))\n        finally:\n            self._file = None\n\n\ndef merge_recordings(call_id: str, caller_path: Path, agent_path: Path, merged_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    result: dict[str, Any] = {\"call_id\": call_id, \"merged\": False}\n    if not caller_path.exists() or not agent_path.exists():\n        result[\"reason\"] = \"source file missing\"\n        return result\n    if caller_path.stat().st_size < 256 or agent_path.stat().st_size < 256:\n        result[\"reason\"] = \"source too short\"\n        return result\n    if not has_command(\"ffmpeg\"):\n        result[\"reason\"] = \"ffmpeg unavailable\"\n        return result\n    merged_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    command = [\n        \"ffmpeg\",\n        \"-y\",\n        \"-hide_banner\",\n        \"-loglevel\",\n        \"warning\",\n        \"-i\",\n        str(caller_path),\n        \"-i\",\n        str(agent_path),\n        \"-filter_complex\",\n        (\n            f\"[0:a]aresample={RECORDING_MERGE_RATE},asetpts=PTS-STARTPTS,volume=1.0[c0];\"\n            f\"[1:a]aresample={RECORDING_MERGE_RATE},asetpts=PTS-STARTPTS,volume=1.0[c1];\"\n            f\"[c0][c1]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest:normalize=0:dropout_transition=0,\"\n            f\"pan=mono|c0=c0,aresample={RECORDING_MERGE_RATE}\"\n        ),\n        \"-ac\",\n        \"1\",\n        \"-ar\",\n        str(RECORDING_MERGE_RATE),\n        \"-c:a\",\n        \"pcm_s16le\",\n        str(merged_path),\n    ]\n    try:\n        proc = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        result[\"reason\"] = f\"ffmpeg error: {exc}\"\n        return result\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        result[\"reason\"] = redact(proc.stderr.strip()[-300:] or \"ffmpeg exited non-zero\")\n        return result\n    if not merged_path.exists() or merged_path.stat().st_size < 256:\n        result[\"reason\"] = \"merged file missing or empty\"\n        return result\n    result[\"merged\"] = True\n    result[\"bytes\"] = merged_path.stat().st_size\n    return result\n\n\ndef recording_path(kind: str, call_id: str) -> Path:\n    safe_id = re.sub(r\"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]\", \"_\", call_id)\n    return RECORDINGS_DIR / f\"{kind}-{safe_id}.wav\""
}

{

{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "            assert process.stdout is not None\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                chunk = process.stdout.read(INPUT_CHUNK_BYTES_16K)\n                if not chunk:\n                    if process.poll() is not None:\n                        break\n                    continue\n                rms = pcm16_rms(chunk)\n                speaking = rms >= BARGE_IN_RMS_THRESHOLD\n                if speaking:\n                    STATE.update(\"conversation\", last_user_activity_at=utc_now())\n                self.handle_barge_in(speaking)\n                self.put_latest(self.input_queue, chunk)\n                STATE.update(\n                    \"audio\",\n                    caller_audio_active=speaking,\n                    input_chunks=STATE.snapshot()[\"audio\"][\"input_chunks\"] + 1,\n                    input_queue_depth=self.input_queue.qsize(),\n                    last_input_rms=rms,\n                )",

}

{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "    def stop(self) -> None:\n        if self.stop_event.is_set():\n            return\n        self.stop_event.set()\n        STATE.event(\"conversation\", f\"stopping realtime bridge for call {self.call_id}\")\n        for process in list(self.processes):\n            if process.poll() is None:\n                process.terminate()\n        STATE.update(\n            \"conversation\",\n            state=\"ended\",\n            ended_at=utc_now(),\n            turn=\"idle\",\n            call_id=None,\n        )\n        STATE.update(\"audio\", state=\"stopped\", caller_audio_active=False, assistant_audio_active=False)\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", connected=False, state=\"disconnected\")",
  "newString": "    def stop(self) -> None:\n        if self.stop_event.is_set():\n            return\n        self.stop_event.set()\n        STATE.event(\"conversation\", f\"stopping realtime bridge for call {self.call_id}\")\n        for process in list(self.processes):\n            if process.poll() is None:\n                process.terminate()\n        caller_bytes = self.caller_writer.close()\n        agent_bytes = self.agent_writer.close()\n        self.ended_at = utc_now()\n        STATE.update(\n            \"conversation\",\n            state=\"ended\",\n            ended_at=self.ended_at,\n            turn=\"idle\",\n            call_id=None,\n        )\n        STATE.update(\"audio\", state=\"stopped\", caller_audio_active=False, assistant_audio_active=False)\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", connected=False, state=\"disconnected\")\n        threading.Thread(target=self._finalize_recording, args=(caller_bytes, agent_bytes), daemon=True).start()\n\n    def _finalize_recording(self, caller_bytes: int, agent_bytes: int) -> None:\n        result = merge_recordings(self.call_id, self.caller_path, self.agent_path, self.merged_path)\n        record = {\n            \"call_id\": self.call_id,\n            \"started_at\": self.started_at,\n            \"ended_at\": self.ended_at or utc_now(),\n            \"caller\": wav_info(self.caller_path, caller_bytes),\n            \"agent\": wav_info(self.agent_path, agent_bytes),\n            \"merged\": wav_info(self.merged_path, result.get(\"bytes\", 0) if result.get(\"merged\") else 0),\n            \"merge\": result,\n        }\n        with recordings_lock:\n            RECORDINGS.append(record)\n            if len(RECORDINGS) > RECORDINGS_RETENTION:\n                stale = RECORDINGS[:-RECORDINGS_RETENTION]\n                del RECORDINGS[:-RECORDINGS_RETENTION]\n                for old in stale:\n                    for path in (old.get(\"caller\", {}).get(\"path\"), old.get(\"agent\", {}).get(\"path\"), old.get(\"merged\", {}).get(\"path\")):\n                        if path:\n                            try:\n                                Path(path).unlink(missing_ok=True)\n                            except OSError:\n                                pass\n        if result.get(\"merged\"):\n            STATE.event(\"recording\", f\"recording merged: {self.merged_path.name} ({result.get('bytes')} bytes)\")\n        else:\n            STATE.event(\"recording\", f\"recording merge skipped: {result.get('reason', 'unknown')}\")"
}

Now I need to add `wav_info`, `RECORDINGS`, `recordings_lock`, and a `scan_existing_recordings` helper. Let me add them near the `file_info` helper:
editcompletedopt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "def file_info(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return {\"exists\": False}\n    stat = path.stat()\n    return {\n        \"exists\": True,\n        \"bytes\": stat.st_size,\n        \"modified\": datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime, timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }",
  "newString": "def file_info(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    if not path.exists():\n        return {\"exists\": False}\n    stat = path.stat()\n    return {\n        \"exists\": True,\n        \"bytes\": stat.st_size,\n        \"modified\": datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime, timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n    }\n\n\nrecordings_lock = threading.RLock()\nRECORDINGS: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n\n\ndef wav_info(path: Path, expected_bytes: int = 0) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    info: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"path\": str(path),\n        \"filename\": path.name,\n        \"url\": f\"/recordings/{path.name}\",\n        \"exists\": path.exists(),\n    }\n    size = path.stat().st_size if path.exists() else expected_bytes\n    info[\"bytes\"] = size\n    if path.exists():\n        stat = path.stat()\n        info[\"modified\"] = datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime, timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        duration = 0.0\n        try:\n            with wave.open(str(path), \"rb\") as handle:\n                frames = handle.getnframes()\n                rate = handle.getframerate() or 1\n                duration = frames / float(rate)\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        info[\"duration_seconds\"] = round(duration, 2)\n    return info\n\n\ndef scan_existing_recordings() -> None:\n    if not RECORDINGS_DIR.exists():\n        return\n    with recordings_lock:\n        RECORDINGS.clear()\n        groups: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n        for path in RECORDINGS_DIR.iterdir():\n            if not path.is_file() or not path.suffix == \".wav\":\n                continue\n            name = path.stem\n            kind, _, call_id = name.partition(\"-\")\n            if kind not in (\"caller\", \"agent\", \"merged\"):\n                continue\n            entry = groups.setdefault(call_id, {\"call_id\": call_id, \"_paths\": {}})\n            entry[\"_paths\"][kind] = path\n            entry.setdefault(\"started_at\", datetime.fromtimestamp(path.stat().st_mtime, timezone.utc).isoformat())\n            entry[\"ended_at\"] = datetime.fromtimestamp(path.stat().st_mtime, timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        for call_id, entry in groups.items():\n            paths = entry.pop(\"_paths\")\n            record = {\n                \"call_id\": call_id,\n                \"started_at\": entry.get(\"started_at\"),\n                \"ended_at\": entry.get(\"ended_at\"),\n                \"caller\": wav_info(paths[\"caller\"]) if \"caller\" in paths else {\"exists\": False, \"filename\": f\"caller-{call_id}.wav\", \"url\": f\"/recordings/caller-{call_id}.wav\"},\n                \"agent\": wav_info(paths[\"agent\"]) if \"agent\" in paths else {\"exists\": False, \"filename\": f\"agent-{call_id}.wav\", \"url\": f\"/recordings/agent-{call_id}.wav\"},\n                \"merged\": wav_info(paths[\"merged\"]) if \"merged\" in paths else {\"exists\": False, \"filename\": f\"merged-{call_id}.wav\", \"url\": f\"/recordings/merged-{call_id}.wav\"},\n            }\n            RECORDINGS.append(record)\n        RECORDINGS.sort(key=lambda r: r.get(\"ended_at\") or r.get(\"started_at\") or \"\", reverse=True)\n        if len(RECORDINGS) > RECORDINGS_RETENTION:\n            stale = RECORDINGS[RECORDINGS_RETENTION:]\n            del RECORDINGS[RECORDINGS_RETENTION:]\n            for old in stale:\n                for info in (old.get(\"caller\"), old.get(\"agent\"), old.get(\"merged\")):\n                    path_str = info.get(\"path\") if isinstance(info, dict) else None\n                    if path_str:\n                        try:\n                            Path(path_str).unlink(missing_ok=True)\n                        except OSError:\n                            pass"
}

Now update the HTTP handler to serve recordings:
editcompletedopt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def do_GET(self) -> None:\n        if self.path not in (\"/\", \"/status.json\"):\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        data = status()\n        if self.path == \"/status.json\":\n            body = json.dumps(data, indent=2).encode()\n            content_type = \"application/json; charset=utf-8\"\n        else:\n            body = render_page(data)\n            content_type = \"text/html; charset=utf-8\"\n        self.send_response(200)\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", content_type)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-store\")\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Length\", str(len(body)))\n        self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body)",
  "newString": "class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def do_GET(self) -> None:\n        parsed = urlparse(self.path)\n        if parsed.path == \"/recordings\" or parsed.path.startswith(\"/recordings/\"):\n            self.serve_recording(parsed.path)\n            return\n        if parsed.path not in (\"/\", \"/status.json\"):\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        data = status()\n        if parsed.path == \"/status.json\":\n            body = json.dumps(data, indent=2).encode()\n            content_type = \"application/json; charset=utf-8\"\n        else:\n            body = render_page(data)\n            content_type = \"text/html; charset=utf-8\"\n        self.send_response(200)\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", content_type)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-store\")\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Length\", str(len(body)))\n        self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body)\n\n    def serve_recording(self, path: str) -> None:\n        relative = path[len(\"/recordings/\"):] if path != \"/recordings\" else \"\"\n        if not relative or \"/\" in relative or \"..\" in relative:\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        filename = unquote(relative)\n        target = (RECORDINGS_DIR / filename).resolve()\n        try:\n            target.relative_to(RECORDINGS_DIR.resolve())\n        except ValueError:\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        if not target.exists() or not target.is_file():\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        try:\n            body = target.read_bytes()\n        except OSError:\n            self.send_error(404)\n            return\n        self.send_response(200)\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"audio/wav\")\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Length\", str(len(body)))\n        self.send_header(\"Content-Disposition\", f'inline; filename=\"{filename}\"')\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-store\")\n        self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body)"
}
Output exit=
Edit applied successfully.

{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "def ensure_runtime(env: dict[str, str]) -> None:\n    if not has_command(\"baresip\"):\n        raise SystemExit(\"baresip is required\")\n    if not has_command(\"ffmpeg\"):\n        raise SystemExit(\"ffmpeg is required\")\n    run([\"modprobe\", \"snd-aloop\"], timeout=5)\n    if genai is None:\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"dependency_missing\", last_error=\"google-genai is not installed\")\n    elif env.get(\"GEMINI_API_KEY\") or os.environ.get(\"GEMINI_API_KEY\"):\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"configured\")\n    else:\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"not_configured\", last_error=\"GEMINI_API_KEY missing\")",
  "newString": "def ensure_runtime(env: dict[str, str]) -> None:\n    if not has_command(\"baresip\"):\n        raise SystemExit(\"baresip is required\")\n    if not has_command(\"ffmpeg\"):\n        raise SystemExit(\"ffmpeg is required\")\n    RECORDINGS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    scan_existing_recordings()\n    run([\"modprobe\", \"snd-aloop\"], timeout=5)\n    if genai is None:\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"dependency_missing\", last_error=\"google-genai is not installed\")\n    elif env.get(\"GEMINI_API_KEY\") or os.environ.get(\"GEMINI_API_KEY\"):\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"configured\")\n    else:\n        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"not_configured\", last_error=\"GEMINI_API_KEY missing\")"
}

minimax-m3-free | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
Now add the `render_recordings` function. Let me add it next to `render_cards`:
editcompletedopt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": "def render_cards(section: dict[str, Any], wanted: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str:\n    cards = []\n    for key, label in wanted:\n        value = section.get(key)\n        cls = badge_class(str(value)) if key == \"state\" else \"neutral\"\n        cards.append(\n            f\"<section class='card {cls}'><div class='label'>{html.escape(label)}</div>\"\n            f\"<div class='value'>{html.escape(str(value))}</div></section>\"\n        )\n    return \"\".join(cards)",
  "newString": "def render_cards(section: dict[str, Any], wanted: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str:\n    cards = []\n    for key, label in wanted:\n        value = section.get(key)\n        cls = badge_class(str(value)) if key == \"state\" else \"neutral\"\n        cards.append(\n            f\"<section class='card {cls}'><div class='label'>{html.escape(label)}</div>\"\n            f\"<div class='value'>{html.escape(str(value)))}</div></section>\"\n        )\n    return \"\".join(cards)\n\n\ndef format_bytes(value: Any) -> str:\n    try:\n        size = int(value)\n    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n        return \"n/a\"\n    if size < 1024:\n        return f\"{size} B\"\n    units = [\"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"]\n    for unit in units:\n        size /= 1024.0\n        if size < 1024:\n            return f\"{size:.1f} {unit}\"\n    return f\"{size:.1f} TB\"\n\n\ndef render_recording_track(label: str, info: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    if not info or not info.get(\"exists\"):\n        return (\n            f\"<div class='track missing'>\"\n            f\"<div class='track-label'>{html.escape(label)}</div>\"\n            f\"<div class='track-detail'>not captured</div>\"\n            f\"</div>\"\n        )\n    filename = info.get(\"filename\", \"\")\n    url = info.get(\"url\", \"#\")\n    duration = info.get(\"duration_seconds\")\n    size = format_bytes(info.get(\"bytes\", 0))\n    duration_str = f\"{duration:.1f} s\" if isinstance(duration, (int, float)) and duration else \"n/a\"\n    return (\n        f\"<div class='track'>\"\n        f\"<div class='track-label'>{html.escape(label)}</div>\"\n        f\"<audio controls preload='none' src='{html.escape(url)}'></audio>\"\n        f\"<div class='track-detail'>{html.escape(duration_str)} - {html.escape(size)} - <a href='{html.escape(url)}' download>download</a></div>\"\n        f\"<div class='track-name'>{html.escape(filename)}</div>\"\n        f\"</div>\"\n    )\n\n\ndef render_recordings(recordings: list[dict[str, Any]], recordings_dir: str, retention: int) -> str:\n    header = (\n        f\"<p class='muted'>Stored in <code>{html.escape(recordings_dir)}</code>. \"\n        f\"Most recent {int(retention)} calls retained. Each call produces three WAV tracks \"\n        f\"(caller microphone, Gemini assistant audio, merged mix).</p>\"\n    )\n    if not recordings:\n        return header + \"<p class='muted'>No recordings yet. Place a call to extension \" + html.escape(EXTENSION) + \" to generate one.</p>\"\n    items = []\n    for record in recordings:\n        call_id = record.get(\"call_id\", \"?\")\n        started = record.get(\"started_at\") or \"?\"\n        ended = record.get(\"ended_at\") or \"?\"\n        caller = render_recording_track(\"Caller\", record.get(\"caller\") or {})\n        agent = render_recording_track(\"Agent (Gemini)\", record.get(\"agent\") or {})\n        merged = render_recording_track(\"Merged mix\", record.get(\"merged\") or {})\n        merge_status = \"ok\" if (record.get(\"merged\") or {}).get(\"exists\") else \"pending\"\n        items.append(\n            f\"<section class='recording'>\"\n            f\"<header><div><b>Call {html.escape(str(call_id))}</b></div>\"\n            f\"<div class='recording-meta'>started {html.escape(started)}<br>ended {html.escape(ended)} - merge: {html.escape(merge_status)}</div></header>\"\n            f\"<div class='tracks'>{caller}{agent}{merged}</div>\"\n            f\"</section>\"\n        )\n    return header + \"\".join(items)"
}
Output exit=
Edit applied successfully.

{
  "filePath": "/opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py",
  "oldString": ".label {{ color:var(--muted); font-size:.76rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.09em; }} .value {{ margin-top:8px; overflow-wrap:anywhere; font-size:1.05rem; }} .panel {{ margin-top:18px; padding:18px; }} pre {{ overflow:auto; white-space:pre-wrap; word-break:break-word; background:#070910; border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:14px; padding:14px; max-height:520px; }} a {{ color:#a8c7ff; }} ul.events {{ list-style:none; padding:0; margin:0; }} .events li {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:210px 90px 1fr; gap:10px; padding:8px 0; border-bottom:1px solid var(--line); }} .events span,.events b {{ color:var(--muted); }}",
  "newString": ".label {{ color:var(--muted); font-size:.76rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.09em; }} .value {{ margin-top:8px; overflow-wrap:anywhere; font-size:1.05rem; }} .panel {{ margin-top:18px; padding:18px; }} pre {{ overflow:auto; white-space:pre-wrap; word-break:break-word; background:#070910; border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:14px; padding:14px; max-height:520px; }} a {{ color:#a8c7ff; }} ul.events {{ list-style:none; padding:0; margin:0; }} .events li {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:210px 90px 1fr; gap:10px; padding:8px 0; border-bottom:1px solid var(--line); }} .events span,.events b {{ color:var(--muted); }}\n.recording {{ border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:14px; padding:14px; margin-bottom:14px; background:rgba(7,9,16,.55); }}\n.recording header {{ display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:flex-start; gap:10px; margin-bottom:10px; flex-wrap:wrap; }}\n.recording-meta {{ color:var(--muted); font-size:.82rem; text-align:right; }}\n.tracks {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(220px,1fr)); gap:12px; }}\n.track {{ background:rgba(21,25,36,.9); border:1px solid var(--line); border-radius:12px; padding:12px; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:6px; }}\n.track.missing {{ opacity:.55; border-style:dashed; }}\n.track-label {{ color:#dce5ff; font-weight:700; font-size:.95rem; }}\n.track audio {{ width:100%; }}\n.track-detail {{ color:var(--muted); font-size:.78rem; overflow-wrap:anywhere; }}\n.track-name {{ color:#6f7d96; font-family:ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; font-size:.72rem; overflow-wrap:anywhere; }}\n.muted {{ color:var(--muted); }}"
}

I don't need to also re-render the recordings panel on every 2-second poll (the page is server-rendered on each GET anyway, but the JS doesn't re-fetch the page). Let me add a periodic full-page reload, or simpler: add a refresh link/button. Actually the simplest approach: just have the page reload itself every 10 seconds, OR add a manual refresh.

Actually, since the recordings are appended on call end and the page can be refreshed manually, I'll add a small periodic full-page reload (every 15 seconds) and also keep the 2-second JSON poll for live state.

Actually, let me reconsider. Full page reload is heavy. A better approach: just leave the panel static and rely on the user clicking refresh. Or do a meta refresh every 30s. Let me just add a manual refresh button next to the recordings heading.

Path traversal protection works. Let me also do a quick unit test of the merge function with synthetic files:
bashcompletedUnit test merge_recordings▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'EOF'\nimport sys, os, wave, struct, subprocess\nsys.path.insert(0, '/opt/conversation-sip')\nimport conversation_app as ca\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ncaller = Path('/tmp/opencode/test-caller.wav')\nagent = Path('/tmp/opencode/test-agent.wav')\nmerged = Path('/tmp/opencode/test-merged.wav')\n\n# Create 1-second sine wave at different rates\ndef write_sine(path, rate, freq=440, dur=1.0, amp=12000):\n    with wave.open(str(path), 'wb') as w:\n        w.setnchannels(1)\n        w.setsampwidth(2)\n        w.setframerate(rate)\n        import math\n        n = int(rate * dur)\n        for i in range(n):\n            sample = int(amp * math.sin(2 * math.pi * freq * i / rate))\n            w.writeframesraw(struct.pack('<h', sample))\n\ncaller.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\nwrite_sine(caller, 16000, freq=440)\nwrite_sine(agent, 24000, freq=660)\n\nresult = ca.merge_recordings('test-001', caller, agent, merged)\nprint('merge result:', result)\nprint('merged exists:', merged.exists(), 'size:', merged.stat().st_size if merged.exists() else 0)\n\n# Check duration of merged\nif merged.exists():\n    with wave.open(str(merged), 'rb') as w:\n        print('merged rate:', w.getframerate(), 'frames:', w.getnframes(), 'duration:', w.getnframes()/w.getframerate())\n        data = w.readframes(w.getnframes())\n        rms = ca.pcm16_rms(data)\n        print('merged rms:', rms)\nEOF",
  "description": "Unit test merge_recordings"
}

A
Assistant
minimax-m3-free | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
Registration worked, but the dial via ctrl_tcp failed: "NETSTRING_ERROR_NO_LENGTH". This is because `nc -q 3 127.0.0.1 4444` may have closed the connection before sending the length-prefixed netstring properly, OR the dial never reached the call.

Let me check the previous successful test logs to see exactly how the dial was sent. ▶ Reasoning
The previous successful test used `token='opencode'` and a different caller prompt that included the dial properly. The bare `nc` approach with simple line output may not send a proper netstring. The previous test must have used Python with proper netstring framing.

Let me use a Python helper to send a proper netstring:

A netstring format is: `len:data,`
So for `{"command":"dial","params":"9762","token":"test01"}` (50 chars), the netstring is `50:{"command":"dial","params":"9762","token":"test01"},`

Let me build a Python helper.
bashcompletedSend dial via proper netstring▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PYEOF'\nimport socket\n\ndef send_ctrl(cmd, params=\"\", token=\"test01\"):\n    body = '{\"command\":\"%s\",\"params\":\"%s\",\"token\":\"%s\"}' % (cmd, params, token)\n    ns = '%d:%s,' % (len(body), body)\n    print('Sending netstring:', ns)\n    s = socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1', 4444), timeout=5)\n    s.sendall(ns.encode())\n    try:\n        s.settimeout(2)\n        data = s.recv(2048)\n        print('Reply:', data.decode(errors='replace'))\n    except socket.timeout:\n        pass\n    s.close()\n\nsend_ctrl('dial', '9762')\nPYEOF",
  "description": "Send dial via proper netstring"
}
Output exit=1
Sending netstring: 51:{"command":"dial","params":"9762","token":"test01"},

  "content": "#!/bin/bash\n# Start a test caller for 9320 in background, wait for ready, then exit\nDIR=\"/tmp/opencode/rec-test\"\nLOG=\"$DIR/caller.log\"\npkill -9 -f \"baresip.*$DIR\" 2>/dev/null\nsleep 1\nrm -f \"$LOG\" \"$DIR/uuid\"\ncd \"$DIR\"\nbaresip -4 -f \"$DIR\" -v > \"$LOG\" 2>&1 &\necho $! > \"$DIR/caller.pid\"\ndisown\nexit 0\n",

Actually the script uses `nohup` and `disown` but the PID file is empty. The issue is that `$!` returns the PID of the backgrounded process, but with `disown`, the variable should still be set. Let me check the script:

```
baresip -4 -f "$DIR" -v > "$LOG" 2>&1 &
echo $! > "$DIR/caller.pid"
disown
exit 0
```

Self-test shows:
1. Inbound test: ok (rms=2586)
2. Outbound test: failed because `hw:Loopback,1,1` is busy (because the conversation-sip service is using it). This is expected — the self-test runs while the service is running and the service has the playback device open.

The self-test failure is a known limitation when run against a live service that already owns the ALSA devices. The actual end-to-end test (the 9320 call) just succeeded with real audio flowing, so this is a non-issue.
