The new detector would have sent 10 turns from the 69s real call instead of 15 tiny splits. That is still a lot, but critically it will send an explicit `audio_stream_end` for each, which native VAD failed to do.
bashcompletedCompare VAD silence hold lengths▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport wave, struct, math\np='/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/caller-1780672141-3659.wav'\nfor hold in [10,12,15,18]:\n  with wave.open(p,'rb') as w:\n    rate=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(w.getnframes())\n  chunk_frames=int(rate*0.1); chunk_bytes=chunk_frames*2\n  segments=[]; active=False; speech_count=0; silence=0; start=None; last_speech=None\n  for idx in range(0,len(data),chunk_bytes):\n    d=data[idx:idx+chunk_bytes]\n    if len(d)<2: break\n    t=idx/2/rate\n    vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d)\n    rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals)))\n    speaking=rms>=500\n    if not active:\n      if speaking: speech_count+=1\n      else: speech_count=0\n      if speech_count>=2:\n        active=True; silence=0; start=max(0,t-(2+3-1)*0.1); last_speech=t+0.1\n      continue\n    if speaking:\n      silence=0; last_speech=t+0.1\n    else:\n      silence+=1\n      if silence>=hold:\n        segments.append((start,t+0.1,last_speech)); active=False; speech_count=0; silence=0\n  if active: segments.append((start,len(data)/2/rate,last_speech))\n  print('hold',hold,'segments',len(segments),[(round(a,1),round(b,1)) for a,b,_ in segments])\nPY",
  "timeout": 10000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Compare VAD silence hold lengths"
}

I’m going to prove the intended API behavior with the latest real caller audio outside SIP: segment it into 3 natural utterances and send each utterance to the same Gemini Live session with `audio_stream_end=True`. If this produces 3 generations, the API is fine and the service state machine fix should work.
bashcompletedProbe Gemini multi-turn with real audio▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport asyncio, wave, struct, math, time, sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom google import genai\nfrom google.genai import types\n\ndef load_env(path):\n    env={}\n    for line in Path(path).read_text().splitlines():\n        line=line.strip()\n        if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n            k,v=line.split('=',1); env[k]=v.strip('\"\\'')\n    return env\n\ndef segment(data, rate, th=500, min_speech=2, hold=15, preroll=3):\n    chunk_frames=int(rate*0.1); cb=chunk_frames*2\n    active=False; speech=0; silence=0; start=None; last=None; segs=[]\n    for idx in range(0,len(data),cb):\n        d=data[idx:idx+cb]\n        if len(d)<2: break\n        t=idx//2//chunk_frames\n        vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d)\n        rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals)))\n        sp=rms>=th\n        if not active:\n            if sp: speech+=1\n            else: speech=0\n            if speech>=min_speech:\n                active=True; silence=0; start=max(0, idx-(min_speech+preroll-1)*cb); last=idx+cb\n            continue\n        if sp:\n            silence=0; last=idx+cb\n        else:\n            silence+=1\n            if silence>=hold:\n                segs.append((start, idx+cb, last)); active=False; speech=0; silence=0\n    if active: segs.append((start,len(data),last))\n    return segs\n\np='/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/caller-1780672141-3659.wav'\nwith wave.open(p,'rb') as w:\n    rate=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(w.getnframes())\nsegs=segment(data,rate)\nprint('segments', [(round(a/2/rate,1), round(b/2/rate,1)) for a,b,_ in segs])\nenv=load_env('/opt/conversation-sip/.env')\nclient=genai.Client(api_key=env['GEMINI_API_KEY'])\n\nasync def wait_generation(session, turn, timeout=12):\n    t0=time.time(); out=''; inp=''; audio=0\n    async for msg in session.receive():\n        sc=getattr(msg,'server_content',None)\n        if not sc: continue\n        if getattr(sc,'input_transcription',None) and getattr(sc.input_transcription,'text',''):\n            inp += sc.input_transcription.text\n        if getattr(sc,'output_transcription',None) and getattr(sc.output_transcription,'text',''):\n            out += sc.output_transcription.text\n        mt=getattr(sc,'model_turn',None)\n        if mt and mt.parts:\n            for part in mt.parts:\n                inline=getattr(part,'inline_data',None)\n                if inline and inline.data: audio += len(inline.data)\n        if getattr(sc,'generation_complete',False):\n            print('turn', turn, 'dt', round(time.time()-t0,2), 'input', repr(inp), 'output', repr(out), 'audio', audio)\n            return True\n        if time.time()-t0 > timeout:\n            print('turn', turn, 'timeout partial', repr(inp), repr(out), audio)\n            return False\n\nasync def main():\n    config=types.LiveConnectConfig(\n        response_modalities=[types.Modality.AUDIO],\n        system_instruction='Du bist ein knapper, freundlicher Telefonassistent. Antworte immer auf Deutsch in einem kurzen Satz.',\n        speech_config=types.SpeechConfig(language_code='de-DE'),\n        input_audio_transcription=types.AudioTranscriptionConfig(),\n        output_audio_transcription=types.AudioTranscriptionConfig(),\n        thinking_config=types.ThinkingConfig(thinking_level=types.ThinkingLevel.MINIMAL),\n        realtime_input_config=types.RealtimeInputConfig(\n            activity_handling=types.ActivityHandling.START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS,\n            turn_coverage=types.TurnCoverage.TURN_INCLUDES_ONLY_ACTIVITY,\n            automatic_activity_detection=types.AutomaticActivityDetection(\n                start_of_speech_sensitivity=types.StartSensitivity.START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,\n                end_of_speech_sensitivity=types.EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,\n                silence_duration_ms=350,\n                prefix_padding_ms=100,\n            ),\n        ),\n    )\n    async with client.aio.live.connect(model=env['GEMINI_MODEL'], config=config) as session:\n        for i,(a,b,last) in enumerate(segs[:3],1):\n            chunk=3200\n            sent=0\n            for off in range(a,b,chunk):\n                await session.send_realtime_input(audio=types.Blob(data=data[off:off+chunk], mime_type='audio/pcm;rate=16000'))\n                sent += 1\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.01)\n            await session.send_realtime_input(audio_stream_end=True)\n            print('sent turn', i, 'chunks', sent)\n            ok=await wait_generation(session,i)\n            if not ok: break\nasyncio.run(main())\nPY",
  "timeout": 60000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Probe Gemini multi-turn with real audio"
}

{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport asyncio, wave, struct, math, time\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom google import genai\nfrom google.genai import types\n\ndef load_env(path):\n    env={}\n    for line in Path(path).read_text().splitlines():\n        line=line.strip()\n        if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:\n            k,v=line.split('=',1); env[k]=v.strip('\"\\'')\n    return env\n\ndef segment(data, rate, th=500, min_speech=2, hold=10, preroll=3):\n    chunk_frames=int(rate*0.1); cb=chunk_frames*2\n    active=False; speech=0; silence=0; start=None; segs=[]\n    for idx in range(0,len(data),cb):\n        d=data[idx:idx+cb]\n        if len(d)<2: break\n        vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d)\n        rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals)))\n        sp=rms>=th\n        if not active:\n            if sp: speech+=1\n            else: speech=0\n            if speech>=min_speech:\n                active=True; silence=0; start=max(0, idx-(min_speech+preroll-1)*cb)\n            continue\n        if sp:\n            silence=0\n        else:\n            silence+=1\n            if silence>=hold:\n                segs.append((start, idx+cb)); active=False; speech=0; silence=0\n    if active: segs.append((start,len(data)))\n    return segs\n\nwith wave.open('/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/caller-1780672141-3659.wav','rb') as w:\n    rate=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(w.getnframes())\nsegs=segment(data,rate)\nprint('segments', [(round(a/2/rate,1), round(b/2/rate,1)) for a,b in segs[:5]])\nenv=load_env('/opt/conversation-sip/.env')\nclient=genai.Client(api_key=env['GEMINI_API_KEY'])\n\nasync def recv_one(session, turn):\n    t0=time.time(); inp=''; out=''; audio=0\n    async for msg in session.receive():\n        sc=getattr(msg,'server_content',None)\n        if not sc: continue\n        if getattr(sc,'input_transcription',None) and getattr(sc.input_transcription,'text',''):\n            inp += sc.input_transcription.text\n        if getattr(sc,'output_transcription',None) and getattr(sc.output_transcription,'text',''):\n            out += sc.output_transcription.text\n        mt=getattr(sc,'model_turn',None)\n        if mt and mt.parts:\n            for part in mt.parts:\n                inline=getattr(part,'inline_data',None)\n                if inline and inline.data: audio += len(inline.data)\n        if getattr(sc,'generation_complete',False):\n            print('GEN', turn, 'after', round(time.time()-t0,2), 'input', repr(inp), 'output', repr(out), 'audio', audio)\n            return True\n        if time.time()-t0>10:\n            print('TIMEOUT', turn, repr(inp), repr(out), audio)\n            return False\n\nasync def main():\n    config=types.LiveConnectConfig(\n        response_modalities=[types.Modality.AUDIO],\n        system_instruction='Du bist ein knapper, freundlicher Telefonassistent. Antworte immer auf Deutsch in einem kurzen Satz.',\n        speech_config=types.SpeechConfig(language_code='de-DE'),\n        input_audio_transcription=types.AudioTranscriptionConfig(),\n        output_audio_transcription=types.AudioTranscriptionConfig(),\n        thinking_config=types.ThinkingConfig(thinking_level=types.ThinkingLevel.MINIMAL),\n        realtime_input_config=types.RealtimeInputConfig(\n            activity_handling=types.ActivityHandling.START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS,\n            turn_coverage=types.TurnCoverage.TURN_INCLUDES_ONLY_ACTIVITY,\n            automatic_activity_detection=types.AutomaticActivityDetection(\n                start_of_speech_sensitivity=types.StartSensitivity.START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,\n                end_of_speech_sensitivity=types.EndSensitivity.END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH,\n                silence_duration_ms=350,\n                prefix_padding_ms=100,\n            ),\n        ),\n    )\n    async with client.aio.live.connect(model=env['GEMINI_MODEL'], config=config) as session:\n        for i,(a,b) in enumerate(segs[:3],1):\n            chunks=0\n            for off in range(a,b,3200):\n                await session.send_realtime_input(audio=types.Blob(data=data[off:off+3200], mime_type='audio/pcm;rate=16000'))\n                chunks += 1\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n            await session.send_realtime_input(audio_stream_end=True)\n            print('sent',i,'chunks',chunks)\n            if not await recv_one(session,i): break\nasyncio.run(main())\nPY",
  "timeout": 60000,
  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Probe Gemini shorter real turns"
}

I’m adding one more guard: a max local utterance duration, so a long monologue cannot leave Gemini waiting forever. It will only force-end on a silence chunk, not mid-sample.

{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n                 self.vad_silence_chunks = 0\n                 self.vad_last_speech_time = now\n+                self.vad_turn_started_at = now\n                 self.vad_turns += 1\n@@\n         self.vad_silence_chunks += 1\n         silence_long_enough = (\n             self.vad_silence_chunks >= VAD_HOLD_CHUNKS\n             and (now - self.vad_last_speech_time) >= VAD_SILENCE_DEBOUNCE\n         )\n-        if silence_long_enough:\n+        max_turn_reached = (now - self.vad_turn_started_at) >= VAD_MAX_TURN_SECONDS\n+        if silence_long_enough or max_turn_reached:\n             self.vad_user_speaking = False\n             self.vad_speech_chunks = 0\n             self.last_local_turn_end_at = now\n             STATE.update(\"conversation\", turn=\"listening\")\n-            STATE.event(\"vad\", f\"activity_end after {self.vad_silence_chunks} silent chunks\")\n+            reason = \"max duration\" if max_turn_reached and not silence_long_enough else f\"{self.vad_silence_chunks} silent chunks\"\n+            STATE.event(\"vad\", f\"activity_end after {reason}\")\n             self.enqueue_gemini(\"end\")\n*** End Patch"
}

**Setting up the test caller**

I'm thinking about how I need to set up a test caller. I could reuse code, which might save some time. I want to create a temporary caller and maybe use Text-to-Speech (TTS). The existing prompt is in English, but the output is still in German. For two-turns, I need to use the same prompt twice. I must ensure that credentials are not printed, so I'll check that I don't leak any secrets while using cached data and write it out. I’ll execute this using bash with Python.
Service is in the new mode: `local_utterance_stream_end`. I’m creating a temporary SIP caller and recording what it receives.
bashcompletedPrepare SIP test caller▶

  "workdir": "/",

}

▶ Reasoning
**Updating configuration for audio**

I need to update the configuration source to support two turns for the audio prompt. The plan is to create a two-turn audio sequence, using the same initial prompt, adding a 3-second silence before repeating the prompt for the second part. If the first turn ends around 4.4 seconds, the second should begin after that 3-second pause. I also need to adjust the incoming path for this setup.
One-turn path is now good: German response, audible caller-side audio, `audio_stream_end` latency 405ms after local end. Running two-turn test now.

Two-turn SIP test now produces `generation_complete_count: 2`, `vad_turns: 2`, caller-side audio is audible, and the agent WAV grew to 3.6s. But I see another source of confusion: the agent WAV currently stores Gemini audio back-to-back without timeline silence, so recordings can sound like one short blob. I’m going to fix recordings so the agent track preserves call timing/silence and the merged recording aligns correctly.

A
Assistant
gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
I’m making the agent WAV timeline-preserving now: silence before/between agent turns will be written into the agent track, and it will be padded to the call duration on hangup. That makes the agent recording and merged recording faithful.
apply_patchcompletedSuccess. Updated the following files:
M opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶
Input
{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n     def write(self, pcm: bytes) -> None:\n         if not pcm or self._file is None:\n             return\n         with self.lock:\n             if self._file is None:\n@@\n                 self.error = redact(str(exc))\n                 self._close_unlocked()\n+\n+    def write_silence(self, byte_count: int) -> None:\n+        byte_count -= byte_count % 2\n+        if byte_count <= 0 or self._file is None:\n+            return\n+        block = b\"\\x00\\x00\" * self.sample_rate\n+        with self.lock:\n+            if self._file is None:\n+                return\n+            remaining = byte_count\n+            try:\n+                while remaining > 0:\n+                    chunk = block[: min(len(block), remaining)]\n+                    self._file.writeframesraw(chunk)\n+                    self.bytes_written += len(chunk)\n+                    remaining -= len(chunk)\n+            except Exception as exc:\n+                self.error = redact(str(exc))\n+                self._close_unlocked()\n*** End Patch"
}
Output exit=
Success. Updated the following files:
M opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py

Adding timeline padding hooks in the bridge.
apply_patchcompletedSuccess. Updated the following files:
M opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶
Input
{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n         self.caller_writer = WavWriter(self.caller_path, RECORDING_CALLER_RATE)\n         self.agent_writer = WavWriter(self.agent_path, RECORDING_AGENT_RATE)\n         self.started_at = utc_now()\n+        self.started_monotonic = time.monotonic()\n         self.ended_at: str | None = None\n@@\n     def stop(self) -> None:\n         if self.stop_event.is_set():\n             return\n         self.stop_event.set()\n         STATE.event(\"conversation\", f\"stopping realtime bridge for call {self.call_id}\")\n         for process in list(self.processes):\n             if process.poll() is None:\n                 process.terminate()\n+        self.pad_agent_recording_to_now()\n         caller_bytes = self.caller_writer.close()\n         agent_bytes = self.agent_writer.close()\n@@\n         if result.get(\"merged\"):\n             STATE.event(\"recording\", f\"recording merged: {self.merged_path.name} ({result.get('bytes')} bytes)\")\n         else:\n             STATE.event(\"recording\", f\"recording merge skipped: {result.get('reason', 'unknown')}\")\n+\n+    def pad_agent_recording_to_now(self) -> None:\n+        elapsed = max(0.0, time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic)\n+        target_bytes = int(elapsed * RECORDING_AGENT_RATE) * 2\n+        self.agent_writer.write_silence(target_bytes - self.agent_writer.bytes_written)\n+\n+    def write_agent_recording(self, pcm: bytes) -> None:\n+        self.pad_agent_recording_to_now()\n+        self.agent_writer.write(pcm)\n*** End Patch"
}

A
Assistant
gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
The new two-turn test revealed the next real bug: when turn 2 starts while turn 1’s response is still being generated/played, Gemini often does not generate the second answer. The fix is to serialize turns: after we send `audio_stream_end`, wait for `generation_complete` before sending the next buffered user utterance. For interruptions, we still collect the caller audio and suppress/clear the old assistant playback.
apply_patchcompletedSuccess. Updated the following files:
M opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶

}

{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n         queue_ref = self.gemini_input_queue\n         if queue_ref is None:\n             return\n         stream_ended = False\n+        awaiting_generation = False\n+        generation_wait_started = 0.0\n         while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n+            if awaiting_generation:\n+                done = self.gemini_generation_done\n+                if done is not None and done.is_set():\n+                    awaiting_generation = False\n+                elif time.monotonic() - generation_wait_started > 8.0:\n+                    STATE.event(\"gemini\", \"generation wait timed out; sending next queued turn\")\n+                    awaiting_generation = False\n+                else:\n+                    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n+                    continue\n             try:\n                 item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue_ref.get(), timeout=0.5)\n             except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                 continue\n             if isinstance(item, str):\n                 if item == \"start\":\n                     stream_ended = False\n                     STATE.update(\"gemini\", last_event=\"activity_start\")\n                 elif item == \"end\":\n                     if not stream_ended:\n                         try:\n+                            done = self.gemini_generation_done\n+                            if done is not None:\n+                                done.clear()\n                             await session.send_realtime_input(audio_stream_end=True)\n                             STATE.update(\"gemini\", last_event=\"audio_stream_end\")\n                             stream_ended = True\n+                            awaiting_generation = True\n+                            generation_wait_started = time.monotonic()\n                         except Exception as exc:\n                             STATE.update(\"gemini\", last_error=redact(str(exc))[:200], last_event=\"stream_end_error\")\n                 continue\n*** End Patch"
}

▶ Reasoning
**Considering session management**

I’m thinking about how to handle interruptions effectively. There might be a risk of timing out, so perhaps implementing an instrument is necessary. One option could be to close the current Gemini session and start a new one when the user interrupts, making the process more deterministic. But I also wonder if a simpler approach would work: if a barge-in is detected, just start a new Gemini session after the current generation is complete.
The two-turn prompt overlapped the second caller utterance with the first assistant response, so it exercised barge-in and still did not produce turn 2. I’m isolating whether plain non-overlapping multi-turn works by increasing the gap between the two test utterances.

> /tmp/opencode/fix-test/caller.pid\nsleep 6\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport socket, time\nbody = '{\"command\":\"dial\",\"params\":\"9762\",\"token\":\"fixgap\"}'\nns = ('%d:%s,' % (len(body), body)).encode(); s=socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1',4447), timeout=5); s.sendall(ns); time.sleep(.3)\ntry: print(s.recv(2048).decode(errors='replace')[:120])\nexcept Exception as exc: print('no reply', exc)\ns.close()\nPY\nsleep 30\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport json, math, struct, subprocess, wave\nfrom pathlib import Path\nagent=sorted(Path('/opt/conversation-sip/recordings').glob('agent-*.wav'), key=lambda p:p.stat().st_mtime)[-1]\nwith wave.open(str(agent),'rb') as w:\n    n=w.getnframes(); r=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(n)\nprint(agent.name, 'dur', round(n/r,2), 'bytes', agent.stat().st_size)\ncb=int(r*.1)*2; segs=[]; start=None; last=None\nfor i in range(0,len(data),cb):\n    d=data[i:i+cb]\n    if len(d)<2: break\n    vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d); rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals))); t=i/2/r\n    if rms>250:\n        if start is None: start=t\n        last=t+.1\n    else:\n        if start is not None: segs.append((start,last)); start=None\nif start is not None: segs.append((start,last))\nprint('agent_non_silent_segments', [(round(a,1),round(b,1)) for a,b in segs])\nstatus=json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['curl','-s','http://127.0.0.1:8096/status.json']))\ng=status['state']['gemini']; a=status['state']['audio']\nprint('gemini', {k:g.get(k) for k in ['generation_complete_count','vad_turns','audio_chunks_sent','audio_chunks_dropped','input_transcript','output_transcript','last_error']})\nprint('audio', {k:a.get(k) for k in ['barge_in_count','output_chunks','last_error']})\nfor e in status['state']['events'][-20:]: print(f\"{e['at']} [{e['kind']}] {e['message'][:140]}\")\nPY",

gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
**Planning audio processing**

I see I need to work with 5-8 seconds of audio and resample to 8k using `ffmpeg` in bash. The command sounds alright. I'll first compose a prompt, include 5 seconds of silence, then add the second German part and finish with a tail. I realize I don't necessarily need file read operations for generating audio, but the instructions suggest using `apply_patch` for edits, so I'll use Python to avoid that. I'll read the 24k segment, then decimate every third sample to achieve 8k. Sounds doable!
The repeated-prompt test can be misleading because Gemini may treat the second identical utterance as duplicate/no-op. I’m creating a two-turn test where the second utterance is different real speech, using a prior German agent phrase resampled as caller audio.
bashcompletedRun two different utterances test▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 - <<'PY'\nimport pathlib, time, wave, struct\nbase=pathlib.Path('/tmp/opencode/fix-test')\n# Extract audible agent response from timeline WAV and downsample 24k -> 8k by taking every third sample.\nagent='/opt/conversation-sip/recordings/agent-1780673474-7948.wav'\nsecond='/tmp/opencode/fix-test/second-german-8k.wav'\nwith wave.open(agent,'rb') as w:\n    r=w.getframerate(); samples=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*w.getnframes(), w.readframes(w.getnframes()))\nstart=int(5.0*r); end=int(8.0*r)\nseg=samples[start:end]\nseg8=seg[::3]\nwith wave.open(second,'wb') as w:\n    w.setnchannels(1); w.setsampwidth(2); w.setframerate(8000)\n    w.writeframes(struct.pack('<'+'h'*len(seg8), *seg8))\nfirst='/tmp/opencode/conversation-caller-9320/caller-prompt-8k.wav'\nout='/tmp/opencode/fix-test/two-different.wav'\nwith wave.open(first,'rb') as r:\n    audio1=r.readframes(r.getnframes())\nwith wave.open(second,'rb') as r:\n    audio2=r.readframes(r.getnframes())\nwith wave.open(out,'wb') as w:\n    w.setnchannels(1); w.setsampwidth(2); w.setframerate(8000)\n    w.writeframes(audio1); w.writeframes(b'\\x00\\x00'*8000*6); w.writeframes(audio2); w.writeframes(b'\\x00\\x00'*8000*10)\nincoming=f'/tmp/opencode/fix-test/incoming-diff-{int(time.time())}.wav'\nbase.joinpath('incoming_path').write_text(incoming)\nconfig=base.joinpath('config').read_text().splitlines()\nnew=[]\nfor line in config:\n    if line.startswith('audio_source\\t'): new.append(f'audio_source\\taufile,{out}')\n    elif line.startswith('audio_player\\t'): new.append(f'audio_player\\taufile,{incoming}')\n    else: new.append(line)\nbase.joinpath('config').write_text('\\n'.join(new)+'\\n')\nfor p in [second,out]:\n    with wave.open(p,'rb') as w: print(p, round(w.getnframes()/w.getframerate(),2))\nprint('incoming', incoming)\nPY\noldpid=$(cat /tmp/opencode/fix-test/caller.pid 2>/dev/null); [ -n \"$oldpid\" ] && kill -9 \"$oldpid\" 2>/dev/null || true\nsleep 1\nsetsid nohup baresip -4 -f /tmp/opencode/fix-test -v < /dev/null > /tmp/opencode/fix-test/caller.log 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/opencode/fix-test/caller.pid\nsleep 6\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport socket, time\nbody = '{\"command\":\"dial\",\"params\":\"9762\",\"token\":\"fixdiff\"}'\nns = ('%d:%s,' % (len(body), body)).encode(); s=socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1',4447), timeout=5); s.sendall(ns); time.sleep(.3)\ntry: print(s.recv(2048).decode(errors='replace')[:120])\nexcept Exception as exc: print('no reply', exc)\ns.close()\nPY\nsleep 31\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport json, math, struct, subprocess, wave\nfrom pathlib import Path\nincoming=Path('/tmp/opencode/fix-test/incoming_path').read_text().strip()\nagent=sorted(Path('/opt/conversation-sip/recordings').glob('agent-*.wav'), key=lambda p:p.stat().st_mtime)[-1]\nfor p in [Path(incoming), agent]:\n    with wave.open(str(p),'rb') as w:\n        n=w.getnframes(); r=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(n)\n    vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(data)//2), data) if data else []\n    rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals))) if vals else 0\n    peak=max((abs(v) for v in vals), default=0)\n    print(p.name, 'rate', r, 'dur', round(n/r,2), 'rms', rms, 'peak', peak, 'bytes', p.stat().st_size)\n    cb=int(r*.1)*2; segs=[]; start=None; last=None\n    for i in range(0,len(data),cb):\n        d=data[i:i+cb]\n        if len(d)<2: break\n        vals2=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d); rr=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals2)/len(vals2))); t=i/2/r\n        if rr>250:\n            if start is None: start=t\n            last=t+.1\n        else:\n            if start is not None: segs.append((start,last)); start=None\n    if start is not None: segs.append((start,last))\n    print('  segments', [(round(a,1),round(b,1)) for a,b in segs])\nstatus=json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['curl','-s','http://127.0.0.1:8096/status.json']))\ng=status['state']['gemini']; a=status['state']['audio']\nprint('gemini', {k:g.get(k) for k in ['generation_complete_count','vad_turns','audio_chunks_sent','audio_chunks_dropped','input_transcript','output_transcript','last_error']})\nprint('audio', {k:a.get(k) for k in ['barge_in_count','output_chunks','last_error']})\nfor e in status['state']['events'][-20:]: print(f\"{e['at']} [{e['kind']}] {e['message'][:140]}\")\nPY",

gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
**Addressing audio output issues**

I’m noticing that despite having 69 output chunks, the agent is segmenting just one response. The issue seems to be that the output chunks aren’t resetting at the start of each call. I need to ensure all counters reset properly to track audio responses accurately. The second utterance didn’t generate audio, possibly because it was the agent's response. I need a patch to reset states at the start of the conversation and log generation completion for better tracking. Let’s sort that out!
I found another accounting bug: dashboard counters like `generation_complete_count`, `output_chunks`, and `barge_in_count` were not reset per call. That made several test results ambiguous. I’m resetting per-call metrics and logging every generation completion so the next test is unambiguous.

**Considering sender events**

I think I need to create events for the start and end of sender chunks, which could be helpful. Maybe I should add a `sender_turn` variable, setting it to 0 in the `gemini_sender` as well as `turn_chunks` also starting at 0. When a chunk starts, I can increment that event; if audio is occurring, I’d increase `turn_chunks`. Finally, when the chunk ends, I could send out an event with that count. It seems like a good patch!

A
Assistant
gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
Root cause is now clear: with `audio_stream_end=True`, the Live receive stream can finish after the first generation, which leaves the later queued turns with no active receiver/session. I’m switching to a deterministic per-utterance Live session: collect one caller utterance, open Gemini Live, send that utterance, stream the response, close, then process the next utterance. This avoids the “one response only” failure mode.

{
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py\n@@\n     async def gemini_async(self, api_key: str) -> None:\n         client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)\n         system_instruction = self.env.get(\n             \"GEMINI_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION\",\n             \"Du bist ein knapper, freundlicher Telefonassistent. \"\n@@\n                 ),\n             ),\n         )\n         self.gemini_loop_ref = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n         self.gemini_input_queue = asyncio.Queue(maxsize=1200)\n-        self.gemini_generation_done = asyncio.Event()\n-        self.gemini_generation_done.set()\n         STATE.update(\n             \"gemini\",\n-            state=\"connecting\",\n+            state=\"ready\",\n+            connected=False,\n             model=GEMINI_MODEL,\n             language_code=GEMINI_LANGUAGE_CODE,\n             last_error=None,\n         )\n+        STATE.event(\"gemini\", f\"ready for per-utterance Live sessions ({GEMINI_MODEL}, German speech)\")\n         try:\n-            async with client.aio.live.connect(model=GEMINI_MODEL, config=config) as session:\n-                STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"connected\", connected=True, last_event=\"setup_complete\")\n-                STATE.update(\"conversation\", turn=\"listening\")\n-                STATE.event(\"gemini\", f\"connected to {GEMINI_MODEL} (local utterance end, German speech)\")\n-                sender = asyncio.create_task(self.gemini_sender(session))\n-                receiver = asyncio.create_task(self.gemini_receiver(session))\n-                done, pending = await asyncio.wait(\n-                    {sender, receiver}, return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED\n-                )\n-                for task in pending:\n-                    task.cancel()\n-                for task in done:\n-                    exc = task.exception()\n-                    if exc and not self.stop_event.is_set():\n-                        raise exc\n+            await self.gemini_turn_loop(client, config)\n         except Exception as exc:\n             if not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                 snap = STATE.snapshot()\n                 STATE.update(\n                     \"gemini\",\n@@\n         finally:\n             self.gemini_input_queue = None\n             self.gemini_loop_ref = None\n             self.gemini_generation_done = None\n+\n+    async def gemini_turn_loop(self, client: Any, config: Any) -> None:\n+        queue_ref = self.gemini_input_queue\n+        if queue_ref is None:\n+            return\n+        turn_number = 0\n+        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n+            try:\n+                item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue_ref.get(), timeout=0.5)\n+            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n+                continue\n+            if item != \"start\":\n+                continue\n+            turn_number += 1\n+            chunks: list[bytes] = []\n+            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n+                try:\n+                    item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue_ref.get(), timeout=0.5)\n+                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n+                    continue\n+                if item == \"end\":\n+                    break\n+                if isinstance(item, bytes):\n+                    chunks.append(item)\n+            if chunks:\n+                await self.run_gemini_turn(client, config, turn_number, chunks, self.last_local_turn_end_at)\n+\n+    async def run_gemini_turn(self, client: Any, config: Any, turn_number: int, chunks: list[bytes], turn_end_at: float) -> None:\n+        self.suppress_agent_output = False\n+        self.response_turn_end_at = turn_end_at\n+        STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"connecting\", connected=False, last_event=\"turn_connecting\")\n+        STATE.event(\"gemini\", f\"turn {turn_number}: opening Live session with {len(chunks)} chunks\")\n+        try:\n+            async with client.aio.live.connect(model=GEMINI_MODEL, config=config) as session:\n+                STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"connected\", connected=True, last_event=\"setup_complete\")\n+                for chunk in chunks:\n+                    if self.stop_event.is_set():\n+                        return\n+                    await session.send_realtime_input(audio=types.Blob(data=chunk, mime_type=\"audio/pcm;rate=16000\"))\n+                    self.vad_audio_chunks_sent += 1\n+                    STATE.update(\n+                        \"gemini\",\n+                        state=\"listening\",\n+                        last_event=\"audio_input\",\n+                        audio_chunks_sent=self.vad_audio_chunks_sent,\n+                        audio_chunks_dropped=self.gemini_sender_dropped,\n+                    )\n+                    await asyncio.sleep(0.005)\n+                await session.send_realtime_input(audio_stream_end=True)\n+                STATE.update(\"gemini\", last_event=\"audio_stream_end\")\n+                STATE.event(\"gemini\", f\"turn {turn_number}: audio_stream_end sent\")\n+                async for message in session.receive():\n+                    if self.stop_event.is_set():\n+                        break\n+                    generation_complete = await self.handle_gemini_message(message)\n+                    if generation_complete:\n+                        break\n+        except Exception as exc:\n+            if not self.stop_event.is_set():\n+                STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"error\", connected=False, last_error=redact(str(exc)))\n+                STATE.event(\"error\", f\"Gemini turn {turn_number} failed: {exc}\")\n+        finally:\n+            STATE.update(\"gemini\", connected=False)\n*** End Patch"
}

**Updating response handling**

I need to define `self.response_turn_end_at` because it wasn’t set during initialization. I’m thinking of making that change as a patch. It’s a little oversight on my part, but fixing it will ensure that everything functions smoothly moving forward!

s=socket.create_connection(('127.0.0.1',4447), timeout=5); s.sendall(ns); time.sleep(.3)\ntry: print(s.recv(2048).decode(errors='replace')[:120])\nexcept Exception as exc: print('no reply', exc)\ns.close()\nPY\nsleep 34\npython3 - <<'PY'\nimport json, math, struct, subprocess, wave\nfrom pathlib import Path\nincoming=Path('/tmp/opencode/fix-test/incoming_path').read_text().strip()\nagent=sorted(Path('/opt/conversation-sip/recordings').glob('agent-*.wav'), key=lambda p:p.stat().st_mtime)[-1]\nfor p in [Path(incoming), agent]:\n    with wave.open(str(p),'rb') as w:\n        n=w.getnframes(); r=w.getframerate(); data=w.readframes(n)\n    vals=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(data)//2), data) if data else []\n    rms=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals)/len(vals))) if vals else 0\n    peak=max((abs(v) for v in vals), default=0)\n    print(p.name, 'rate', r, 'dur', round(n/r,2), 'rms', rms, 'peak', peak, 'bytes', p.stat().st_size)\n    cb=int(r*.1)*2; segs=[]; start=None; last=None\n    for i in range(0,len(data),cb):\n        d=data[i:i+cb]\n        if len(d)<2: break\n        vals2=struct.unpack('<'+'h'*(len(d)//2), d); rr=int(math.sqrt(sum(v*v for v in vals2)/len(vals2))); t=i/2/r\n        if rr>250:\n            if start is None: start=t\n            last=t+.1\n        else:\n            if start is not None: segs.append((start,last)); start=None\n    if start is not None: segs.append((start,last))\n    print('  segments', [(round(a,1),round(b,1)) for a,b in segs])\nstatus=json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['curl','-s','http://127.0.0.1:8096/status.json']))\ng=status['state']['gemini']; a=status['state']['audio']\nprint('gemini', {k:g.get(k) for k in ['generation_complete_count','vad_turns','audio_chunks_sent','audio_chunks_dropped','first_audio_latency_ms','input_transcript','output_transcript','last_event','last_error']})\nprint('audio', {k:a.get(k) for k in ['barge_in_count','output_chunks','last_error']})\nfor e in status['state']['events'][-35:]: print(f\"{e['at']} [{e['kind']}] {e['message'][:160]}\")\nPY",

  "workdir": "/",
  "description": "Verify per-turn Gemini sessions"

gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
**Updating streaming function**

I need to replace `gemini_turn_loop` with `run_gemini_streaming_turn` at the start and pass the queue. This function opens the session and then continuously reads from the queue, sending chunks until it reaches "end." Meanwhile, the receiver task runs concurrently to handle incoming data. I'm wondering if it’s necessary to check if the receiver completes before the end, but it doesn’t seem likely to be a concern.
Per-utterance sessions fixed correctness but introduced latency because I was replaying the whole utterance after the caller stopped. I’m changing that now: open the per-turn Live session at speech start and stream audio as it arrives, then send `audio_stream_end` at speech end. That keeps correctness and gets latency back down.

M opt/conversation-sip/conversation_app.py▶

turn_number, queue_ref)\n+\n+    async def run_gemini_streaming_turn(self, client: Any, config: Any, turn_number: int, queue_ref: asyncio.Queue) -> None:\n         self.suppress_agent_output = f\"turn {turn_number}: opening Live session with {len(chunks)} chunks\")\n+        STATE.event(\"gemini\", f\"turn {turn_number}: opening Live session\")\n         try:\n             async with client.aio.live.connect(model=GEMINI_MODEL, config=config) as session:\n                 STATE.update(\"gemini\", state=\"connected\", connected=True, last_event=\"setup_complete\")\n- mime_type=\"audio/pcm;rate=16000\"))\n-                    self.vad_audio_chunks_sent += 1\n-                    STATE.update(\n-                        \"gemini\",\n-                        state=\"listening\",\n-                        last_event=\"audio_input\",\n-                        audio_chunks_sent=self.vad_audio_chunks_sent,\n-                        audio_chunks_dropped=self.gemini_sender_dropped,\n-                    )\n-                    await asyncio.sleep(0.005)\n+\n+                generation_done = asyncio.Event()\n+\n+                async def receive_turn() -> None:\n+                    async for message in session.receive():\n+                        if self.stop_event.is_set():\n+                            break\n+ generation_done.set()\n+\n+                receiver_task = asyncio.create_task(receive_turn())\n+                turn_chunks = 0\n+                while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n+ mime_type=\"audio/pcm;rate=16000\"))\n+                    turn_chunks += 1\n+                    self.vad_audio_chunks_sent += 1\n+                    STATE.update(\n+                        \"gemini\",\n+                        state=\"listening\",\n+                        last_event=\"audio_input\",\n+                        audio_chunks_sent=self.vad_audio_chunks_sent,\n+ {
  "patchText": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /opt/conversation-sip/README.md\n@@\n-- Gemini Live native server-side VAD handles turn boundaries; local VAD is only used for dashboard state and barge-in detection.\n+- Local VAD detects caller utterances and sends each completed utterance to a fresh Gemini Live session. This avoids the Developer API's single-response behavior after `audio_stream_end` while still streaming each utterance in real time.\n - Local barge-in detection clears queued assistant audio when caller speech is detected while Gemini is speaking.\n+- Agent recordings are timeline-preserving: silence is written before and between Gemini turns so the agent track aligns with the caller and merged tracks.\n*** End Patch"
}

A
Assistant
gpt-5.5 | agent=build | finish=stop
I reworked the core design.

Root cause:
- The Gemini Developer API Live session often behaved like one response after `audio_stream_end`.
- Native continuous VAD also failed on real calls.
- Recordings were misleading because the agent track used to concatenate Gemini audio without timeline silence.

What I changed:
- Each caller utterance now gets its own fresh Gemini Live session.
- The session opens at speech start, streams audio in real time, sends `audio_stream_end` at utterance end, receives one response, then closes.
- Local VAD handles utterance boundaries with 1s silence debounce.
- German is still hardcoded with `de-DE`.
- Agent recordings now preserve call timeline, including silence before/between Gemini turns.
- Per-call counters now reset correctly.

Verified:
- Two-turn non-overlap test: `generation_complete_count: 2`, two audible agent regions.
- Barge-in/overlap test: `generation_complete_count: 2`, `barge_in_count: 1`, two audible agent regions.
- First audio latency after utterance end: about `500ms`.
- Service is active, SIP registered, no audio errors.

Try calling again now.
